gold_answer: 2.47 — your business is with action only, never with its fruits; let not the fruit be your motive.
question: Which verse states that one's right is to action alone and never to its fruits, so that the fruit of action should not be the motive?

hi.gold_answer: 2. 47-आपका व्यवसाय केवल कार्य के साथ है, कभी भी उसके फल के साथ नहीं; फल को आपका उद्देश्य न बनने दें।
hi.question: किस श्लोक में कहा गया है कि किसी का अधिकार केवल कार्य करना है और कभी भी उसके फल के लिए नहीं है, ताकि कर्म का फल उद्देश्य न हो?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 47-भवतः व्यवसायः केवलं क्रियया एव भवति, कदापि तस्य फलैः न भवति; फलः भवतः हेतुः न भवेत्।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् केवलं क्रियायाः अधिकारः अस्ति, कदापि तस्य फलानां अधिकारः नास्ति, येन कर्मफलस्य हेतुः न भवेत्?

_gold_src.text_en: Thy business is with the action only, never with its fruits; so let not the fruit of action be thy motive, nor be thou to inaction attached.
_gold_src.original: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
_gold_src.translit: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Perform action, O Dhananjaya, dwelling in union with the divine, renouncing attachments and balanced evenly in success and failure: equilibrium is called yoga.
_nearmiss_src.original: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय। सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Sages, united to the Pure Reason, renounce the fruit which action yieldeth, and, liberated from the bonds of birth, they go to the blissful seat.
_nearmiss_src.original: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः। जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥ janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty-anāmayam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Therefore, without attachment, constantly perform action which is duty, for, by performing action without attachment, man verily reacheth the Supreme.
_nearmiss_src.original: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर। असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samāchara asakto hyācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣhaḥ

gold_answer: 11.32 — 'Time am I, laying desolate the world, made manifest to slay mankind.'
question: In which verse does the Lord proclaim himself to be Time, made manifest on earth to slay mankind, so that the assembled warriors are already doomed?

hi.gold_answer: 11.32 -'मैं समय हूँ, दुनिया को उजाड़ कर रहा हूँ, मानव जाति को मारने के लिए प्रकट किया गया।'
hi.question: किस श्लोक में भगवान ने खुद को समय घोषित किया है, जो मानव जाति को मारने के लिए पृथ्वी पर प्रकट किया गया है, ताकि इकट्ठे हुए योद्धा पहले से ही बर्बाद हो जाएं?

sa.gold_answer: 11.32 -'अहम् कालः अस्ति, विश्वं निर्जनं कृत्वा, मानवजातीयं मारयितुं प्रकटीकृतः।'
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके भगवतः स्वयमेव कालः इति घोषयति, मानवसमाजस्य वधाय पृथिव्यां प्रकटितः, येन समागताः योद्धारः पूर्वमेव दण्डिताः भवन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Time am I, laying desolate the world, Made manifest on earth to slay mankind! Not one of all these warriors ranged for strife Escapeth death; thou shalt alone survive.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः। ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha kālo ’smi loka-kṣhaya-kṛit pravṛiddho lokān samāhartum iha pravṛittaḥ ṛite ’pi tvāṁ na bhaviṣhyanti sarve ye ’vasthitāḥ pratyanīkeṣhu yodhāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Therefore stand up! win for thyself renown, Conquer thy foes, enjoy the wealth filled realm, By Me they are already overcome, Be thou the outward cause, left-handed one.
_nearmiss_src.original: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्। मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tasmāt tvam uttiṣhṭha yaśho labhasva jitvā śhatrūn bhuṅkṣhva rājyaṁ samṛiddham mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva nimitta-mātraṁ bhava savya-sāchin

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Drona and Bhîshma and Jayadratha, Karna, and all the other warriors here, Are slain by Me. Destroy them fearlessly. Fight! thou shalt crush thy rivals in the field.
_nearmiss_src.original: द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च कर्णं तथाऽन्यानपि योधवीरान्। मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: droṇaṁ cha bhīṣhmaṁ cha jayadrathaṁ cha karṇaṁ tathānyān api yodha-vīrān mayā hatāṁs tvaṁ jahi mā vyathiṣhṭhā yudhyasva jetāsi raṇe sapatnān

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Like Time's destroying flames I see Thy teeth, Upstanding, spread within expanded jaws; Nought know I anywhere, no shelter find; Mercy, O God! re of all the worlds!
_nearmiss_src.original: दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि। दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: danṣhṭrā-karālāni cha te mukhāni dṛiṣhṭvaiva kālānala-sannibhāni diśho na jāne na labhe cha śharma prasīda deveśha jagan-nivāsa

gold_answer: 4.7 — whenever there is decay of righteousness and a rise of unrighteousness, the Lord manifests.
question: Which verse declares that whenever righteousness decays and unrighteousness rises, the Lord himself comes forth?

hi.gold_answer: 4. 7-जब भी धार्मिकता का क्षय और अन्याय का उदय होता है, तो भगवान प्रकट होते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक घोषणा करता है कि जब भी धार्मिकता क्षय होती है और अन्याय बढ़ता है, तो भगवान स्वयं प्रकट होते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 7-यदा धार्म्मिकतायाः क्षयः, अधार्मिकतायाः वृद्धिः च भवति तदा प्रभुः प्रकाशयति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः घोषयति यत् यदा धार्म्मिकं क्षयते तथा अनीतिः वर्धते तदा प्रभुः स्वयमेव बहिः आगच्छति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Whenever there is decay of righteousness, O Bhârata, and there is exaltation of unrighteousness, then I Myself come forth;
_gold_src.original: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛijāmyaham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of evil-doers, for the sake of firmly establishing righteousness, I am born from age to age.
_nearmiss_src.original: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām dharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Though unborn, the imperishable Self , and also the Lord of all beings, brooding over nature which is Mine own, yet I am born through My own Power.
_nearmiss_src.original: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्। प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi san prakṛitiṁ svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who thus knoweth My divine birth and action, in its essence, having abandoned the body, cometh not to birth again, but cometh unto Me, O Arjuna.
_nearmiss_src.original: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः। त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: janma karma cha me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

gold_answer: 4.8 — for the protection of the good and destruction of the wicked, and to re-establish righteousness, he is born age after age.
question: Which verse states that the Lord is born from age to age to protect the good, destroy evil-doers, and re-establish righteousness?

hi.gold_answer: 4. 4-अच्छाई की रक्षा और दुष्टों के विनाश के लिए, और धार्मिकता को फिर से स्थापित करने के लिए, वह उम्र के बाद उम्र में पैदा होता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक बताता है कि भगवान का जन्म युग-दर-युग अच्छे लोगों की रक्षा करने, बुरे लोगों को नष्ट करने और धार्मिकता को फिर से स्थापित करने के लिए हुआ है?

sa.gold_answer: 4. 4-शुभस्य रक्षणाय, दुष्टानां विनाशाय, धार्म्मिकतायै च, सः युगानुयुगे जनयति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् भगवतः युगानुयुगे उत्तमान् रक्षितुं, दुष्टान् नाशयितुं, धार्म्मिकं पुनः प्रतिष्ठापयितुं च जनितः अस्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of evil-doers, for the sake of firmly establishing righteousness, I am born from age to age.
_gold_src.original: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे ॥
_gold_src.translit: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām dharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whenever there is decay of righteousness, O Bhârata, and there is exaltation of unrighteousness, then I Myself come forth;
_nearmiss_src.original: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛijāmyaham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who thus knoweth My divine birth and action, in its essence, having abandoned the body, cometh not to birth again, but cometh unto Me, O Arjuna.
_nearmiss_src.original: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः। त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: janma karma cha me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Though unborn, the imperishable Self , and also the Lord of all beings, brooding over nature which is Mine own, yet I am born through My own Power.
_nearmiss_src.original: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्। प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi san prakṛitiṁ svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā

gold_answer: 1.1 — Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya what the assembled armies did on the holy field of Kuru.
question: Which verse opens the dialogue with the blind king asking what his people and the Pandavas did when gathered, eager for battle, on the field of Kuru?

hi.gold_answer: धृतराष्ट्र ने संजय से पूछा कि एकत्रित सेनाओं ने कुरु के पवित्र मैदान पर क्या किया।
hi.question: किस श्लोक से अंधे राजा के साथ संवाद शुरू होता है जिसमें पूछा जाता है कि उनके लोग और पांडवों ने कुरु के मैदान में युद्ध के लिए उत्सुक होने पर क्या किया?

sa.gold_answer: 1. 1 धृतराष्ट्रः सञ्जयं पृष्टवान् यत् समागताः सेनाः कुरु-पवित्रक्षेत्रे किं कृतवन्तः इति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके अन्धराजेन सह संवादः आरभ्यते, सः पृच्छति यत् तस्य जनाः पाण्डवाः च, युद्धार्थं उत्सुकाः, कुरुक्षेत्रे समागत्यां किं कृतवन्तः इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Dhritarâshtra said: On the holy plain, on the field of Kuru, gathered together, eager for battle, what did they, O Sanjaya, my people and the Pândavas?
_gold_src.original: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः। मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
_gold_src.translit: dhṛitarāśhtra uvācha dharma-kṣhetre kuru-kṣhetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥ māmakāḥ pāṇḍavāśhchaiva kimakurvata sañjaya

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: Having seen arrayed the army of the Pândavas, the Prince Duryodhana approached his teacher, and spake these words:
_nearmiss_src.original: सञ्जय उवाच दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा। आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha dṛiṣhṭvā tu pāṇḍavānīkaṁ vyūḍhaṁ duryodhanastadā āchāryamupasaṅgamya rājā vachanamabravīt

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Gudâkesha, Hrishîkesha, O Bhârata, having stayed that best of chariots in the midst, between the two armies,
_nearmiss_src.original: संजय उवाच एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha evam ukto hṛiṣhīkeśho guḍākeśhena bhārata senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And spake this word to Hrishîkesha, O Lord of Earth: Arjuna said: In the midst, between the two armies, stay my chariot, O Achyuta,
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyuta

gold_answer: 18.78 — where Krishna and Arjuna are, there are prosperity, victory, and happiness.
question: Which closing verse declares that wherever Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, and Arjuna the archer are, there are surely prosperity, victory, and happiness?

hi.gold_answer: 18.78-जहाँ कृष्ण और अर्जुन हैं, वहाँ समृद्धि, विजय और सुख हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा समापन श्लोक घोषणा करता है कि योग के भगवान कृष्ण और तीरंदाज अर्जुन जहाँ भी हैं, वहाँ निश्चित रूप से समृद्धि, विजय और सुख है?

sa.gold_answer: 18.78-यत्र कृष्णः अर्जुनः च सन्ति तत्र समृद्धिः, विजयः, सुखः च सन्ति।
sa.question: योगस्य भगवतः कृष्णः, धनुर्धरः अर्जुनः च यत्र तत्र अवश्यमेव समृद्धिः, विजयः, सुखः च भवति इति कः समापन-श्लोकः घोषयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Wherever is Krishna, Yoga's Lord, wherever is Pârtha, the archer, assured are there prosperity, victory and happiness. So I think.
_gold_src.original: यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः। तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
_gold_src.translit: yatra yogeśhvaraḥ kṛiṣhṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ tatra śhrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Remembering, remembering, also that most marvellous form of Hari, great is my wonder, O King. I rejoice, again and again.
_nearmiss_src.original: तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः। विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tach cha sansmṛitya saṁsmṛitya rūpam aty-adbhutaṁ hareḥ vismayo ye mahān rājan hṛiṣhyāmi cha punaḥ punaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: O King, remembering, remembering this marvellous and holy dialogue between Keshava and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.
_nearmiss_src.original: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम्।केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: rājan sansmṛitya sansmṛitya saṁvādam imam adbhutam keśhavārjunayoḥ puṇyaṁ hṛiṣhyāmi cha muhur muhuḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: I heard this marvellous dialogue of Vâsudeva, and of the great-souled Partha, causing my hair to stand on end.
_nearmiss_src.original: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः।संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha ity ahaṁ vāsudevasya pārthasya cha mahātmanaḥ saṁvādam imam aśhrauṣham adbhutaṁ roma-harṣhaṇam

gold_answer: 8.13 — uttering 'Aum' and remembering the Lord at departure, one goes on the highest path.
question: Which verse teaches that one who departs the body while uttering the single syllable 'Aum' and remembering the Lord attains the highest path?

hi.gold_answer: 8: 13 -'ओम'बोलना और प्रस्थान के समय भगवान का स्मरण करना, व्यक्ति उच्चतम मार्ग पर जाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक सिखाता है कि जो व्यक्ति'ओम'शब्द का उच्चारण करते हुए शरीर छोड़ देता है और भगवान का स्मरण करता है, वह उच्चतम मार्ग प्राप्त करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 8: 13 -'ओम्'इति उच्चार्य, प्रस्थानसमये भगवन्तं स्मृत्य, व्यक्तिः सर्वोच्चमार्गे गच्छति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः शिक्षयति यत् यः'ओम्'इति एकशब्दं उच्चार्य, भगवन्तं स्मृत्य च शरीरम् अपसारयति सः सर्वोच्चमार्गं प्राप्नुयात् इति?

_gold_src.text_en: "Aum!" the one syllabled Eternal , reciting, thinking upon Me, he who goeth forth, abandoning the body, he goeth on the highest path.
_gold_src.original: ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन्। यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: oṁ ityekākṣharaṁ brahma vyāharan mām anusmaran yaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṁ sa yāti paramāṁ gatim

_nearmiss_src.text_en: All the gates closed, the mind confined in the heart, the life-breath fixed in his own head, concentrated by yoga,
_nearmiss_src.original: सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च। मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sarva-dvārāṇi sanyamya mano hṛidi nirudhya cha mūrdhnyādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And he who, casting off the body, goeth forth thinking upon Me only at the time of the end, he entereth into My being: there is no doubt of that.
_nearmiss_src.original: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्। यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: anta-kāle cha mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: In the time of forthgoing, with unshaken mind, fixed in devotion, by the power of yoga drawing together his life-breath in the centre of the two eyebrows, he goeth to this Spirit, supreme, divine.
_nearmiss_src.original: प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव। भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक् स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: prayāṇa-kāle manasāchalena bhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaiva bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyak sa taṁ paraṁ puruṣham upaiti divyam

gold_answer: 6.5 — let a man raise himself by his own Self; the Self is the friend and the enemy of the self.
question: Which verse says that one should raise the self by the Self and not let it sink, since the Self is both the friend and the enemy of the self?

hi.gold_answer: 6. 5-मनुष्य को अपनी आत्मा से स्वयं को ऊपर उठाने दें; आत्मा स्वयं का मित्र और शत्रु है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि व्यक्ति को आत्मा द्वारा आत्म को ऊपर उठाना चाहिए और उसे डूबने नहीं देना चाहिए, क्योंकि आत्मा स्वयं का मित्र और शत्रु दोनों है?

sa.gold_answer: 6. 5-मनुष्यः स्वयमेव आत्मानं उत्थापयेत्; आत्मः आत्मस्य मित्रः शत्रुः च अस्ति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् व्यक्तिः आत्मानं उत्थापयेत्, न निमग्ना भवितुम् अर्हति, यतः आत्मा आत्मस्य मित्रः शत्रुः च अस्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Let him raise the self by the Self and not let the self become depressed; for verily is the Self the friend of the self, and also the Self the self's enemy;
_gold_src.original: उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet ātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Self is the friend of the self of him in whom the self by the Self is vanquished; but to the unsubdued self the Self verily becometh hostile as an enemy.
_nearmiss_src.original: बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः। अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ anātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The higher Self of him who is Self -controlled and peaceful is uniform in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, as well as in honour and dishonour.
_nearmiss_src.original: जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः। शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: jitātmanaḥ praśhāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who regards impartially lovers, friends, and foes, strangers, neutrals, foreigners and relatives, also the righteous and unrighteous, he excelleth.
_nearmiss_src.original: सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु। साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: suhṛin-mitrāryudāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣhya-bandhuṣhu sādhuṣhvapi cha pāpeṣhu sama-buddhir viśhiṣhyate

gold_answer: 7.7 — nothing is higher than the Lord; all is strung on him as pearls on a thread.
question: Which verse states that there is nothing higher than the Lord, and that all this is threaded on him like rows of pearls on a string?

hi.gold_answer: 7: 7 भगवान से बड़ा कुछ भी नहीं है; सब कुछ उस पर धागे पर मोती के रूप में बंधा हुआ है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक बताता है कि भगवान से बड़ा कुछ भी नहीं है, और यह सब एक तार पर मोतियों की पंक्तियों की तरह उन पर थ्रेडेड है?

sa.gold_answer: 7-भगवतः परं किमपि श्रेष्ठं नास्ति; सर्वं तस्य उपरि तन्त्रे मुत्यवत् निबद्धं भवति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् भगवतः श्रेष्ठः किमपि नास्ति इति, तथा च एतत् सर्वं तन्त्रे मुत्यानाम् पङ्क्त्या इव तस्य उपरि योजितम् अस्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: There is naught whatsoever higher than I, O Dhananjaya. All this is threaded on Me, as rows of pearls on a string.
_gold_src.original: मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय। मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
_gold_src.translit: mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjaya mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Know this to be the womb of all beings. I am the source of the forthgoing of the whole universe and likewise the place of its dissolving.
_nearmiss_src.original: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय। अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhāraya ahaṁ kṛitsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: I the sapidity in waters, O son of Kuntî, I the radiance in moon and sun; the Word of Power in all the Vedas, sound in ether, and virility in men;
_nearmiss_src.original: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः। प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śhaśhi-sūryayoḥ praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣhu śhabdaḥ khe pauruṣhaṁ nṛiṣhu

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Know Me, O Pârtha! as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the Reason of the Reason-endowed, the splendour of splendid things am I.
_nearmiss_src.original: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्। बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham

gold_answer: 9.26 — a leaf, flower, fruit, or water offered with devotion is accepted by the Lord.
question: Which verse says the Lord accepts a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water offered to him with devotion?

hi.gold_answer: 9. 26-भक्ति के साथ चढ़ाया गया एक पत्ता, फूल, फल या पानी भगवान द्वारा स्वीकार किया जाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि भगवान भक्ति के साथ उन्हें अर्पित किए गए एक पत्ते, एक फूल, एक फल या पानी को स्वीकार करते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 9. 26-भक्त्या समर्पितं पत्रं, पुष्पं, फलं, जलं वा भगवतः स्वीकरोति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वदति यत् भगवतः तं समर्पितं पत्रं, पुष्पं, फलं, जलं वा भक्त्या स्वीकरोति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: He who offereth to Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, water, that I accept from the striving self, offered as it is with devotion.
_gold_src.original: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति। तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: patraṁ puṣhpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati tadahaṁ bhaktyupahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whatsoever thou doest, whatsoever thou eatest, whatsoever thou offerest, whatsoever thou givest, whatsoever thou doest of austerity, O Kaunteya, do thou that as an offering unto Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्। यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yat karoṣhi yad aśhnāsi yaj juhoṣhi dadāsi yat yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣhva mad-arpaṇam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: They who worship the Shining Ones go to the Shining Ones; to the Ancestors go the Ancestor-worshippers; to the Elements go those who sacrifice to Elementals; but My worshippers come unto Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यान्ति देवव्रता देवान् पितृ़न्यान्ति पितृव्रताः। भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yānti deva-vratā devān pitṝīn yānti pitṛi-vratāḥ bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti mad-yājino ’pi mām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On Me fix thy mind, be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; prostrate thyself before Me; harmonised thus in the Self , thou shalt come unto Me, having Me as thy supreme goal.
_nearmiss_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु। मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ

gold_answer: 10.20 — 'I am the Self seated in the heart of all beings; the beginning, middle, and end of all.'
question: Which verse declares the Lord to be the Self seated in the heart of all beings, the beginning, middle, and end of all beings?

hi.gold_answer: 10.20 -'मैं सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में विराजमान आत्मा हूँ; सभी की शुरुआत, मध्य और अंत।'
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक भगवान को सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में विराजमान आत्मा घोषित करता है, जो सभी प्राणियों की शुरुआत, मध्य और अंत है?

sa.gold_answer: 10.20-"अहं सर्वप्राणिनां हृदये उपविष्टः आत्मा; सर्वस्य आरम्भः, मध्यः, अन्तः च।"
sa.question: कः श्लोकः भगवन्तं सर्वप्राणिनां हृदये उपविष्टः आत्मा इति घोषयति, यः सर्वप्राणिनां आरम्भः, मध्यः, अन्तः च अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: I, O Gudakesha, am the Self , seated in the heart of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and also the end of all beings.
_gold_src.original: अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः। अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥
_gold_src.translit: aham ātmā guḍākeśha sarva-bhūtāśhaya-sthitaḥ aham ādiśh cha madhyaṁ cha bhūtānām anta eva cha

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Of creations the beginning and the ending, and also the middle am I, O Arjuna. Of sciences the science concerning the Self ; the speech of orators I.
_nearmiss_src.original: सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन। अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sargāṇām ādir antaśh cha madhyaṁ chaivāham arjuna adhyātma-vidyā vidyānāṁ vādaḥ pravadatām aham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu; of radiances the glorious sun; I am Marîchi of the Maruts; of the asterisms the Moon am I.
_nearmiss_src.original: आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्। मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ādityānām ahaṁ viṣhṇur jyotiṣhāṁ ravir anśhumān marīchir marutām asmi nakṣhatrāṇām ahaṁ śhaśhī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: I am the Generator of all; all evolves from Me; understanding thus, the wise adore Me in rapt emotion.
_nearmiss_src.original: अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते। इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate iti matvā bhajante māṁ budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ

gold_answer: 15.1 — the imperishable Asvattha tree with roots above and branches below.
question: Which verse describes the eternal Asvattha tree as having its roots above and its branches below?

hi.gold_answer: 15. 1-अविनाशी अश्वत्थ वृक्ष जिसकी जड़ें ऊपर और शाखाएं नीचे होती हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक शाश्वत अश्वथ वृक्ष का वर्णन करता है कि इसकी जड़ें ऊपर और शाखाएं नीचे हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 15. 1-अक्षीयः अश्वत्थवृक्षः यस्य मूलानि उपरि, शाखाः अधः च सन्ति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके शाश्वतस्य अश्वत्थवृक्षस्य वर्णनं कृतम् अस्ति यत् तस्य मूलानि उपरि, शाखाः अधः च सन्ति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: With roots above, branches below, the Asvattha is said to be indestructible; the leaves of it are hymns; he who knoweth it is a Veda-knower.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाचऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śhākham aśhvatthaṁ prāhur avyayam chhandānsi yasya parṇāni yas taṁ veda sa veda-vit

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Downwards and upwards spread the branches of it, nourished by the qualities; the objects of the senses its buds; and its roots grow downwards, the bonds of action in the world of men.
_nearmiss_src.original: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः।अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: adhaśh chordhvaṁ prasṛitās tasya śhākhā guṇa-pravṛiddhā viṣhaya-pravālāḥ adhaśh cha mūlāny anusantatāni karmānubandhīni manuṣhya-loke

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Nor here may be acquired knowledge of its form, nor its end, nor its origin, nor its rooting-place; this strongly rooted Asvattha having been cut down by the unswerving weapon of non-attachment,
_nearmiss_src.original: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा।अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate nānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣhṭhā aśhvattham enaṁ su-virūḍha-mūlam asaṅga-śhastreṇa dṛiḍhena chhittvā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That path beyond may be sought, treading which there is no return. I go indeed to that Primal Man, whence the ancient energy forth-streamed.
_nearmiss_src.original: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्य यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः।तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tataḥ padaṁ tat parimārgitavyaṁ yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ tam eva chādyaṁ puruṣhaṁ prapadye yataḥ pravṛittiḥ prasṛitā purāṇī

gold_answer: 13.2 — the body is the Field, and its knower is the Knower of the Field.
question: Which verse names this body the 'Field' and the one who knows it the 'Knower of the Field'?

hi.gold_answer: 13. 2-शरीर क्षेत्र है, और इसका ज्ञाता क्षेत्र का ज्ञाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक इस शरीर को'क्षेत्र'और जो इसे जानता है उसे'क्षेत्र का जानकार'नाम देता है?

sa.gold_answer: 13. 2 शरीरं क्षेत्रं, तस्य ज्ञाता च क्षेत्रं ज्ञाता।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः अस्य शरीरस्य'क्षेत्रं'इति नामकरणं करोति, यः तत् जानाति सः'क्षेत्रं जानाति'इति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: This body, son of Kuntî, is called the Field; that which knoweth it is called the Knower of the Field by the Sages.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha idaṁ śharīraṁ kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyate etad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Arjuna said: Matter and Spirit, even the Field and the Knower of the Field, wisdom and that which ought to be known, these I fain would learn, O Keshava.
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च। एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva kṣhetraṁ kṣhetra-jñam eva cha etad veditum ichchhāmi jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ cha keśhava

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Understand Me as the Knower of the Field in all Fields, O Bhârata. Wisdom as to the Field and the Knower of the Field, that in My opinion is the wisdom.
_nearmiss_src.original: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत। क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kṣhetra-jñaṁ chāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhārata kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama

_nearmiss_src.text_en: What that Field is and of what nature, how modified, and whence it is, and what He is and what His powers, hear that now briefly from Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tat kṣhetraṁ yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yat sa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu

gold_answer: 17.23 — 'Aum Tat Sat' is the threefold designation of the Eternal.
question: Which verse gives 'Aum Tat Sat' as the threefold designation of the Eternal?

hi.gold_answer: 17.23 -'ओम तत सत'शाश्वत का तीन गुना पदनाम है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक'ओम तत सत'को शाश्वत के तीन पदनाम के रूप में देता है?

sa.gold_answer: 17.23 -'ओम् तात् सत्'इति नित्यस्य त्रिगुण-पदनाम अस्ति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः'ओम् तात् सत्'इति नित्यस्य त्रिगुण-पदनाम प्रददाति?

_gold_src.text_en: " Aum Tat Sat ," this has been considered to be the threefold designation of the Eternal . By that were ordained of old Brâhmanas, Vedas and sacrifices.
_gold_src.original: तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः।ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥
_gold_src.translit: oṁ tat sad iti nirdeśho brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛitaḥ brāhmaṇās tena vedāśh cha yajñāśh cha vihitāḥ purā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Therefore with the pronunciation of " Aum " the acts of sacrifice, gift and austerity as laid down in the ordinances are always commenced by the knowers of the Eternal .
_nearmiss_src.original: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः।प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tasmād oṁ ity udāhṛitya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ pravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṁ brahma-vādinām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: With the pronunciation of " Tat " and without aiming at fruit are performed the various acts of sacrifice, austerity and gift, by those desiring liberation.
_nearmiss_src.original: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः।दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṁ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ dāna-kriyāśh cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: " Sat " is used in the sense of reality and goodness; likewise, O Pârtha, the word " Sat " is used in the sense of a good work.
_nearmiss_src.original: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate praśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate

gold_answer: 16.21 — lust, wrath, and greed are the triple gate of hell; renounce these three.
question: Which verse names lust, wrath, and greed as the threefold gate of hell that a person should renounce?

hi.gold_answer: 16.21-वासना, क्रोध और लालच नरक के तीन द्वार हैं; इन तीनों का त्याग करें।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक वासना, क्रोध और लालच को नरक के तीन द्वार के रूप में नामित करता है जिसे एक व्यक्ति को त्यागना चाहिए?

sa.gold_answer: 16.21-कामः, क्रोधः, लोभः च नरकस्य त्रिविध-द्वाराणि सन्ति; एतान् त्रिन् परित्यज्यतु।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कामम्, क्रोधं, लोभं च नरकस्य त्रिगुण-द्वारम् इति वदति यत् मनुष्यः परित्यक्तुं शक्नोति?

_gold_src.text_en: Triple is the gate of this hell, destructive of the self—lust, wrath, and greed; therefore let man renounce these three.
_gold_src.original: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥
_gold_src.translit: tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśhanam ātmanaḥ kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet

_nearmiss_src.text_en: A man liberated from these three gates of darkness, O son of Kunti, accomplisheth his own welfare, and thus reacheth the highest goal.
_nearmiss_src.original: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः।आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ ācharaty ātmanaḥ śhreyas tato yāti parāṁ gatim

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Cast into demoniacal wombs, deluded birth after birth, attaining not to Me, O Kaunteya, they sink into the lowest depths.
_nearmiss_src.original: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि।मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: āsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Given over to egoism, power, insolence, lust and wrath, these malicious ones hate Me in the bodies of others and in their own.
_nearmiss_src.original: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः।मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ahankāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ cha sanśhritāḥ mām ātma-para-deheṣhu pradviṣhanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ

gold_answer: 11.12 — the radiance of a thousand suns might resemble the glory of that Form.
question: Which verse says that if the splendour of a thousand suns blazed forth at once, it might resemble the glory of that great Form?

hi.gold_answer: 11.12-एक हजार सूर्यों की चमक उस रूप की महिमा के समान हो सकती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि यदि एक हजार सूर्यों की भव्यता एक साथ चमकती है, तो यह उस महान रूप की महिमा के समान हो सकती है?

sa.gold_answer: 11.12-सहस्रस्य सूर्यस्य तेजः तस्य रूपस्य वैभवेन सदृशः भवेत्।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वदति यत् यदि सहस्रस्य सूर्यस्य तेजः एकवारं प्रज्ज्वलितः तर्हि तत् तस्य महान् रूपस्य तेजः सदृशः भवेत् इति?

_gold_src.text_en: If the splendour of a thousand suns were to blaze out together in the sky, that might resemble the glory of that Mahâtman.
_gold_src.original: दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता। यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: divi sūrya-sahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthitā yadi bhāḥ sadṛiśhī sā syād bhāsas tasya mahātmanaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Shining, a mass of splendour everywhere, With discus, mace, tiara, I behold: Blazing as fire, as sun dazzling the gaze, From all sides in the sky, immeasurable.
_nearmiss_src.original: किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च तेजोराशिं सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम्। पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ता द्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kirīṭinaṁ gadinaṁ chakriṇaṁ cha tejo-rāśhiṁ sarvato dīptimantam paśhyāmi tvāṁ durnirīkṣhyaṁ samantād dīptānalārka-dyutim aprameyam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There Pândava beheld the whole universe, divided into manifold parts, standing in one in the body of the Deity of Deities.
_nearmiss_src.original: तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा। अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tatraika-sthaṁ jagat kṛitsnaṁ pravibhaktam anekadhā apaśhyad deva-devasya śharīre pāṇḍavas tadā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Wearing divine necklaces and vestures, anointed with divine unguents, the God all-marvellous, boundless, with face turned everywhere.
_nearmiss_src.original: दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम्। सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: divya-mālyāmbara-dharaṁ divya-gandhānulepanam sarvāśhcharya-mayaṁ devam anantaṁ viśhvato-mukham

gold_answer: 2.20 — the Self is unborn, eternal, and is not slain when the body is slain.
question: Which verse says the dweller in the body is unborn and undying — not slain when the body is slain?

hi.gold_answer: 2. 20-आत्मा अजन्मा, शाश्वत है, और शरीर के मारे जाने पर उसकी हत्या नहीं की जाती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि शरीर में रहने वाला अजन्मा और अमर है-शरीर के मारे जाने पर मारा नहीं जाता है?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 20-आत्मा अजन्मा, नित्यः अस्ति, शरीरस्य वधेन सः न म्रियते।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वदति यत् शरीरस्थः अजन्मा च अमरः अस्ति-शरीरस्य वधेन सः न हतः इति?

_gold_src.text_en: He is not born, nor doth he die; nor having been, ceaseth he any more to be; unborn, perpetual, eternal and ancient, he is not slain when the body is slaughtered.
_gold_src.original: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
_gold_src.translit: na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who regardeth this as a slayer, and he who thinketh he is slain, both of them are ignorant. He slayeth not, nor is he slain.
_nearmiss_src.original: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्। उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaśh chainaṁ manyate hatam ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Who knoweth him indestructible, perpetual, unborn, undiminishing, how can that man slay, O Pârtha, or cause to be slain?
_nearmiss_src.original: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्। कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: vedāvināśhinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam kathaṁ sa puruṣhaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Weapons cleave him not, nor fire burneth him, nor waters wet him, nor wind drieth him away.
_nearmiss_src.original: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः। न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ na chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ

gold_answer: 2.22 — as a man casts off worn garments for new, so the soul takes on new bodies.
question: Which verse compares the soul casting off worn-out bodies and taking new ones to a man discarding worn-out garments for new ones?

hi.gold_answer: 2. 22-जिस प्रकार मनुष्य नए के लिए पुराने कपड़े उतार देता है, उसी प्रकार आत्मा नए शरीर धारण करती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक आत्मा के जीर्ण-शीर्ण शरीरों को त्यागने और नए शरीरों को लेने की तुलना एक ऐसे व्यक्ति से करता है जो नए शरीरों के लिए जीर्ण-शीर्ण वस्त्रों को त्याग देता है?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 22-यथा मनुजः नूतनानां कृते जीर्णवस्त्राणि निष्कासयति, तथा च आत्मा नूतनाः शरीरानि धरति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः मृतशरीराणां त्यागस्य, नूतनानां ग्रहणस्य च आत्मानं, नूतनानां कृते जीर्णवस्त्राणां त्यागस्य पुरुषस्य तुलनां करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: As a man, casting off worn-out garments, taketh new ones, so the dweller in the body, casting off worn-out bodies, entereth into others that are new.
_gold_src.original: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि। तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
_gold_src.translit: vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇi tathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānya nyāni sanyāti navāni dehī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As the dweller in the body experienceth in the body childhood, youth, old age, so passeth he on to another body; the steadfast one grieveth not thereat.
_nearmiss_src.original: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा। तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Weapons cleave him not, nor fire burneth him, nor waters wet him, nor wind drieth him away.
_nearmiss_src.original: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः। न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ na chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Uncleavable he, incombustible he, and indeed neither to be wetted nor dried away; perpetual, all-pervasive, stable, immovable, ancient.
_nearmiss_src.original: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च। नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śhoṣhya eva cha nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ

gold_answer: 6.19 — the steady yogi's mind is like a lamp unflickering in a windless place.
question: Which verse likens the yogi of subdued thought to a lamp that does not flicker in a windless place?

hi.gold_answer: 6. 19-स्थिर योगी का मन हवा रहित स्थान पर प्रज्ज्वलित दीपक की तरह होता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक दमित विचार के योगी की तुलना एक ऐसे दीपक से करता है जो हवा रहित स्थान पर चमकता नहीं है?

sa.gold_answer: 6. 19-स्थिरस्य योगिनः मनः वायुरहितस्थाने प्रज्ज्वलितस्य दीपस्य सदृशः भवति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वशीकृतचिन्तायाः योगिनः वायुहीनस्थाने न प्रज्ज्वलितेन दीपैः सह तुलनां करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: As a lamp in a windless place flickereth not, to such is likened the Yogî of subdued thought, absorbed in the yoga of the Self .
_gold_src.original: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता। योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛitā yogino yata-chittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: When his subdued thought is fixed on the Self , free from longing after all desirable things, then it is said, "he is harmonised."
_nearmiss_src.original: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते। निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yadā viniyataṁ chittam ātmanyevāvatiṣhṭhate niḥspṛihaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ityuchyate tadā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That in which the mind finds rest, quieted by the practice of yoga: that in which he, seeing the Self by the Self , in the Self is satisfied;
_nearmiss_src.original: यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया। यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yatroparamate chittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā yatra chaivātmanātmānaṁ paśhyann ātmani tuṣhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let the Yogî constantly engage himself in yoga, remaining in a secret place by himself, with thought and self subdued, free from hope and greed.
_nearmiss_src.original: योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः। एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ ekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśhīr aparigrahaḥ

gold_answer: 1.47 — Arjuna sinks onto the chariot seat, dropping his bow, his mind overwhelmed by grief.
question: Which verse describes Arjuna sinking down on the seat of his chariot, casting away his bow and arrow, overcome by grief?

hi.gold_answer: 1. 47-अर्जुन अपना धनुष गिराते हुए रथ की सीट पर डूब जाता है, जिससे उसका मन दुःख से अभिभूत हो जाता है।
hi.question: किस श्लोक में अर्जुन के अपने रथ की सीट पर डूबने का वर्णन किया गया है, अपने धनुष और तीर को फेंकते हुए, दुःख से अभिभूत?

sa.gold_answer: 1. 47-अर्जुनः रथस्य आसनस्य उपरि निमग्नः भवति, धनुः पातयति, तस्य चित्तं दुःखेन व्याकुलं भवति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके वर्णितम् अस्ति यत् अर्जुनः रथस्य आसने निमग्नः, स्वस्य धनुः बाणं च अपसार्य, दुःखेन पीडितः इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: Having thus spoken on the battle-field, Arjuna sank down on the seat of the chariot, casting away his bow and arrow, his mind overborne by grief.
_gold_src.original: सञ्जय उवाच एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्। विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśhat visṛijya sa-śharaṁ chāpaṁ śhoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If the sons of Dhritarâshtra, weapon in hand, should slay me, unresisting, unarmed, in the battle, that would for me be the better.
_nearmiss_src.original: यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः। धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥ dhārtarāṣhṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣhemataraṁ bhavet

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Deeply moved to pity, thus uttered in sadness: Arjuna said: Seeing these my kinsmen, O Krishna, arrayed, eager to fight,
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच कृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत्। दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: To him thus with pity overcome, with smarting brimming eyes, despondent, Madhusûdana spake these words:
_nearmiss_src.original: सञ्जय उवाच तं तथा कृपयाऽविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्। विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha taṁ tathā kṛipayāviṣhṭamaśhru pūrṇākulekṣhaṇam viṣhīdantamidaṁ vākyam uvācha madhusūdanaḥ

gold_answer: 2.67 — the mind following the wandering senses is carried off like a boat by the wind.
question: Which verse uses the image of a boat driven off course by the wind on the water to describe how a roving sense carries away the understanding?

hi.gold_answer: 2. 67-भटकती इंद्रियों का अनुसरण करने वाला मन हवा से नाव की तरह बह जाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक पानी पर हवा से बहने वाली नाव की छवि का उपयोग यह वर्णन करने के लिए करता है कि कैसे एक घूमने की भावना समझ को दूर ले जाती है?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 67-भ्रमणं कुर्वतां इन्द्रियानां अनुसरणं कुर्वन् मनम् नौकायाः इव वायुना वहति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः जलस्य वायुना मार्गात् बहिष्कृतस्य नौकायाः चित्रं उपयुञ्जते यत् कथं परिभ्रमणशक्तिः अवबोधं वहति इति वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Such of the roving senses as the mind yieldeth to, that hurries away the understanding, just as the gale hurries away a ship upon the waters.
_gold_src.original: इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते। तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
_gold_src.translit: indriyāṇāṁ hi charatāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate tadasya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi

_nearmiss_src.text_en: O son of Kuntî, the excited senses of even a wise man, though he be striving, impetuously carry away his mind.
_nearmiss_src.original: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः। इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yatato hyapi kaunteya puruṣhasya vipaśhchitaḥ indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There is no Pure Reason for the non-harmonised, nor for the non-harmonised is there concentration; for him without concentration there is no peace, and for the unpeaceful how can there be happiness?
_nearmiss_src.original: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना। न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: nāsti buddhir-ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanā na chābhāvayataḥ śhāntir aśhāntasya kutaḥ sukham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Therefore, O mighty-armed, whose senses are all completely restrained from the objects of sense, of him the understanding is well poised.
_nearmiss_src.original: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛihītāni sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

gold_answer: 2.58 — withdrawing the senses like a tortoise drawing in its limbs.
question: Which verse compares the sage withdrawing his senses from their objects to a tortoise drawing in its limbs?

hi.gold_answer: 2. 58-अपने अंगों में कछुए की तरह इंद्रियों को खींचना।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक उस ऋषि की तुलना करता है जो अपनी इंद्रियों को अपनी वस्तुओं से हटाता है और उसके अंगों में कछुए का चित्र होता है?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 58-कच्छपसदृशं इन्द्रियानां अङ्गानां निष्कासनम्।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके ऋषिः स्व-वस्तुभ्यः इन्द्रियां अपसारयति, तस्य अङ्गानां कच्छप-रेखाङ्कनेन सह तुलनां करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: When, again, as a tortoise draws in on all sides its limbs, he withdraws his senses from the objects of sense, then is his understanding well poised.
_gold_src.original: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_gold_src.translit: yadā sanharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who on every side is without attachments, whatever hap of fair and foul, who neither likes nor dislikes, of such a one the understanding is well poised.
_nearmiss_src.original: यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्। नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śhubhāśhubham nābhinandati na dveṣhṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The objects of sense, but not the relish for them, turn away from an abstemious dweller in the body; and even relish turneth away from him after the Supreme is seen.
_nearmiss_src.original: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः। रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: viṣhayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ rasa-varjaṁ raso ’pyasya paraṁ dṛiṣhṭvā nivartate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Having restrained them all, he should sit harmonised, I his supreme goal; for, whose senses are mastered, of him the understanding is well poised.
_nearmiss_src.original: तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः। वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tāni sarvāṇi sanyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ vaśhe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

gold_answer: 3.21 — people follow whatever a great man does; his conduct sets the standard.
question: Which verse states that whatever a great man does, ordinary people imitate, taking his example as the standard?

hi.gold_answer: 31-एक महान व्यक्ति जो कुछ भी करता है लोग उसका पालन करते हैं; उसका आचरण मानक निर्धारित करता है।
hi.question: किस श्लोक में कहा गया है कि एक महान व्यक्ति जो कुछ भी करता है, आम लोग उसका अनुकरण करते हैं, उसके उदाहरण को मानक के रूप में लेते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 31-महानायकः यत् करोति तत् जनाः अनुसरन्ति, तस्य आचरणं मानदण्डं निर्धारयति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् कश्चन महापुरुषः यत् करोति तत् सामान्यजनाः अनुकरन्ति, तस्य उदाहरणं मानकरूपेण स्वीकरोति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Whatsoever a great man doeth, that other men also do; the standard he setteth up, by that the people go.
_gold_src.original: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः। स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
_gold_src.translit: yad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Janaka and others indeed attained to perfection by action: then having an eye to the welfare of he world also, thou shouldst perform action.
_nearmiss_src.original: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः। लोकसंग्रहमेवापि संपश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasi

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There is nothing in the three worlds, O Pârtha, that should be done by Me, nor anything unattained that might be attained; yet I mingle in action.
_nearmiss_src.original: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन। नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣhu lokeṣhu kiñchana nānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva cha karmaṇi

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As the ignorant act from attachment to action, O Bhârata, so should the wise act without attachment, desiring the welfare of the world.
_nearmiss_src.original: सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत। कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्िचकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: saktāḥ karmaṇyavidvānso yathā kurvanti bhārata kuryād vidvāns tathāsaktaśh chikīrṣhur loka-saṅgraham

gold_answer: 4.34 — learn by reverence, inquiry, and service to the wise who see the truth.
question: Which verse advises learning the truth by humble approach to the wise, by inquiry, and by service?

hi.gold_answer: 4. 4 सत्य को देखने वाले बुद्धिमानों के प्रति सम्मान, जिज्ञासा और सेवा से सीखें।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक बुद्धिमानों के प्रति विनम्र दृष्टिकोण, पूछताछ और सेवा द्वारा सत्य को सीखने की सलाह देता है?

sa.gold_answer: 4. 4 सत्यदर्शनं कुर्वतां ज्ञानिभ्यः श्रद्धया, अन्वेषणेन, सेवया च शिक्षताम्।
sa.question: किं श्लोकः ज्ञानिनां प्रति विनम्रतया, अन्वेषणेन, सेवया च सत्यं ज्ञातुं उपदेशयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Learn thou this by discipleship, by investiga tion, and by service. The wise, the seers of the essence of things, will instruct thee in wisdom.
_gold_src.original: तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया। उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśhnena sevayā upadekṣhyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśhinaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Better than the sacrifice of any objects is the sacrifice of wisdom, O Parantapa. All actions in their entirety, O Pârtha, culminate in wisdom.
_nearmiss_src.original: श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप। सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhreyān dravya-mayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapa sarvaṁ karmākhilaṁ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And having known this, thou shalt not again fall into this confusion, O Pândava; for by this thou wilt see all beings without exception in the Self , and thus in Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव। येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṁ yāsyasi pāṇḍava yena bhūtānyaśheṣheṇa drakṣhyasyātmanyatho mayi

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The man who is full of faith obtaineth wisdom, and he also who hath mastery over his senses; and, having obtained wisdom, he goeth swiftly to the supreme Peace.
_nearmiss_src.original: श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः। ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhraddhāvān labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ sanyatendriyaḥ jñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śhāntim achireṇādhigachchhati

gold_answer: 6.11 — a fixed seat of cloth, antelope skin, and kusha grass, set in a clean place.
question: Which verse describes preparing a firm meditation seat, neither too high nor too low, of cloth, antelope skin, and kusha grass?

hi.gold_answer: 6. 11-कपड़े की एक निश्चित सीट, मृग की त्वचा और कुश घास, एक साफ जगह पर सेट करें।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कपड़े, मृग की त्वचा और कुश घास का एक दृढ़ ध्यान आसन तैयार करने का वर्णन करता है, जो न तो बहुत ऊँचा है और न ही बहुत नीचा है?

sa.gold_answer: 6. 11-वस्त्रस्य, मृगचर्मस्य, कुश-तृणस्य च निश्चितं आसनम्, स्वच्छस्थाने स्थापितम्।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वस्त्रस्य, मृगचर्मस्य, कुश-तृणस्य च न अतिउच्चं, न अत्यल्पं च दृढं ध्यानपीठं सज्जीकर्तुं वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: In a pure place, established on a fixed seat of his own, neither very much raised nor very low, made of a cloth, a black antelope skin, and kusha grass, one over the other;
_gold_src.original: शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः। नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhuchau deśhe pratiṣhṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ nātyuchchhritaṁ nāti-nīchaṁ chailājina-kuśhottaram

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let the Yogî constantly engage himself in yoga, remaining in a secret place by himself, with thought and self subdued, free from hope and greed.
_nearmiss_src.original: योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः। एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ ekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśhīr aparigrahaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There, having made the mind one-pointed, with thought and the functions of the senses subdued, steady on his seat, he should practise yoga for the purification of the self.
_nearmiss_src.original: तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः। उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥ upaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhaye

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Holding the body, head and neck erect, immovably steady, looking fixedly at the point of the nose, with unseeing gaze,
_nearmiss_src.original: समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः। संप्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: samaṁ kāya-śhiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥ samprekṣhya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśhaśh chānavalokayan

gold_answer: 9.22 — to single-minded worshippers, the Lord brings full security (yoga-kshema).
question: Which verse promises that to those who worship the Lord alone with single-minded devotion, he secures their welfare?

hi.gold_answer: 9. 22-एकल विचार वाले उपासकों के लिए, भगवान पूरी सुरक्षा (योग-क्षेमा) लाते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक वादा करता है कि जो लोग एक चित्त भक्ति के साथ केवल भगवान की पूजा करते हैं, वह उनका कल्याण सुनिश्चित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 9. 22-एकमनस्कानां उपासकानां कृते भगवतः पूर्णसुरक्षां (योग-क्षेमं) आनयति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः प्रतिज्ञां करोति यत् ये केवलं एकमनस्कभक्त्या भगवन्तं पूजयन्ति, सः तेषां कल्याणं साधयति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: To those men who worship Me alone thinking of no other, to those ever harmonious, I bring full security.
_gold_src.original: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते। तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: ananyāśh chintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate teṣhāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣhemaṁ vahāmyaham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Even the devotees of other Shining Ones, who worship full of faith, they also worship Me, O son of Kuntî, though contrary to the ancient rule.
_nearmiss_src.original: येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः। तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ye ’pyanya-devatā-bhaktā yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ te ’pi mām eva kaunteya yajantyavidhi-pūrvakam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The same am I to all beings; there is none hateful to Me nor dear. They verily who worship Me with devotion, they are in Me, and I also in them.
_nearmiss_src.original: समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः। ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: samo ’haṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo ’sti na priyaḥ ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpyaham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On Me fix thy mind, be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; prostrate thyself before Me; harmonised thus in the Self , thou shalt come unto Me, having Me as thy supreme goal.
_nearmiss_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु। मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ

gold_answer: 12.13 — the devotee free of ill-will, friendly, compassionate, balanced, and forgiving.
question: Which verse begins the description of the devotee who bears ill-will to no being and is friendly, compassionate, and forgiving?

hi.gold_answer: 12.13-दुर्भावना से मुक्त, मिलनसार, दयालु, संतुलित और क्षमाशील भक्त।
hi.question: किस श्लोक से उस भक्त का वर्णन शुरू होता है जो बिना अस्तित्व के दुर्भावना रखता है और मिलनसार, दयालु और क्षमाशील है?

sa.gold_answer: 12.13-दुर्भावनामुक्तः, मैत्रीपूर्णः, करुणामयः, सन्तुलितः, क्षमाशीलः भक्तः।
sa.question: भक्तस्य वर्णनं कः श्लोकः आरभते यः न सत्तायाः प्रति दुर्भावना धारयति तथा च मैत्रीपूर्णः, करुणामयः, क्षमाशीलः च भवति?

_gold_src.text_en: He who beareth no ill-will to any being, friendly and compassionate, without attachment and egoism, balanced in pleasure and pain, and forgiving,
_gold_src.original: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
_gold_src.translit: adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Ever content, harmonious, with the self-controlled, resolute, with mind and Reason dedicated to Me, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: santuṣhṭaḥ satataṁ yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who wants nothing, is pure, expert, passionless, untroubled, renouncing every undertaking, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: anapekṣhaḥ śhuchir dakṣha udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ sarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who neither loveth nor hateth, nor grieveth, nor desireth, renouncing good and evil, full of devotion, he is dear to Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्ितमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yo na hṛiṣhyati na dveṣhṭi na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati śhubhāśhubha-parityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ

gold_answer: 17.8 — savory, substantial, agreeable foods promoting vitality are dear to the pure.
question: Which verse describes the foods that increase vitality, health, strength, and cheerfulness as dear to the pure (sattvic)?

hi.gold_answer: 17. 8-जीवन शक्ति को बढ़ावा देने वाले स्वादिष्ट, पर्याप्त, स्वादिष्ट खाद्य पदार्थ शुद्ध लोगों को प्रिय होते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक उन खाद्य पदार्थों का वर्णन करता है जो जीवन शक्ति, स्वास्थ्य, शक्ति और प्रफुल्लता को बढ़ाते हैं जो शुद्ध (सात्त्विक) को प्रिय हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 17. 8-प्राणशक्तिं प्रवर्धमानानि रुचिकानि, पर्याप्तानि, अनुकूलानि च खाद्यानि शुद्धेभ्यः प्रियानि भवन्ति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः जीवनशक्तिं, स्वास्थ्यं, शक्तिम्, प्रफुल्लतां च वर्धयतां खाद्यानां वर्णनं शुद्धेभ्यः (सात्विक्) प्रियम् इति करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: The foods that augment vitality, energy, vigour, health, joy and cheerfulness, delicious, bland, substantial and agreeable, are dear to the pure.
_gold_src.original: आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः।रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥
_gold_src.translit: āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛidyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The passionate desire foods that are bitter, sour, saline, over-hot, pungent, dry and burning and which produce pain, grief and sickness.
_nearmiss_src.original: कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः।आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣhṇa- tīkṣhṇa-rūkṣha-vidāhinaḥ āhārā rājasasyeṣhṭā duḥkha-śhokāmaya-pradāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That which is stale and flat, putrid and corrupt, leavings also and unclean, is the food dear to the dark.
_nearmiss_src.original: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत्।उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣhitaṁ cha yat uchchhiṣhṭam api chāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The food also which is dear to each is threefold, as also sacrifice, austerity and almsgiving. Hear thou the distinction of these.
_nearmiss_src.original: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः।यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ yajñas tapas tathā dānaṁ teṣhāṁ bhedam imaṁ śhṛiṇu

gold_answer: 18.66 — abandon all duties and take refuge in the Lord alone; he will free you from all sins.
question: Which verse counsels abandoning all duties and taking sole refuge in the Lord, who promises to free one from all sins?

hi.gold_answer: 18.66-सभी कर्तव्यों को त्याग दें और केवल भगवान की शरण लें; वह आपको सभी पापों से मुक्त कर देगा।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक सभी कर्तव्यों को त्यागने और भगवान में एकमात्र शरण लेने की सलाह देता है, जो सभी पापों से मुक्त होने का वादा करता है?

sa.gold_answer: सर्वकार्याणि परित्यज्य केवलं भगवन्तं शरणम् अगच्छतु, सः सर्वान् पापान् परिहरति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः सर्वान् कर्तव्यान् परित्यज्य सर्वान् पापात् मुक्तुं प्रतिज्ञां कुर्वन् भगवते एकमात्रं आश्रयं ग्रहीतुं उपदेशयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Abandoning all duties come unto Me alone for shelter; sorrow not, I will liberate thee from all sins.
_gold_src.original: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Merge thy mind in Me, be my devotee, sacrifice to Me, prostrate thyself before Me, thou shalt come even to Me. I pledge thee My troth; thou art dear to Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo ‘si me

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Flee unto Him for shelter with all thy being, O Bhârata; by His grace thou shalt obtain supreme peace, the everlasting dwelling-place.
_nearmiss_src.original: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tam eva śharaṇaṁ gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata tat-prasādāt parāṁ śhāntiṁ sthānaṁ prāpsyasi śhāśhvatam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Thus hath wisdom, more secret than secrecy itself, been declared unto thee by Me; having reflected on it fully, then act thou as thou listest.
_nearmiss_src.original: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया।विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: iti te jñānam ākhyātaṁ guhyād guhyataraṁ mayā vimṛiśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru

gold_answer: 10.21 — among the Adityas, Vishnu; among lights, the sun; among stars, the moon.
question: Which verse identifies the Lord, among the Adityas, as Vishnu, and among lights as the radiant sun?

hi.gold_answer: 10.21-आदित्यों के बीच, विष्णु; प्रकाशों के बीच, सूर्य; तारों के बीच, चंद्रमा।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक भगवान की पहचान करता है, आदित्यों में से, विष्णु के रूप में, और प्रकाशों में से उज्ज्वल सूर्य के रूप में?

sa.gold_answer: 10.21-आदित्यानां मध्ये विष्णुः; दीपानां मध्ये सूर्यः; नक्षत्राणां मध्ये चन्द्रः।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः भगवन्तं, आदित्येषु, विष्णुः, दीपेषु च प्रकाशमयः सूर्यः इति सूचयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu; of radiances the glorious sun; I am Marîchi of the Maruts; of the asterisms the Moon am I.
_gold_src.original: आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्। मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥
_gold_src.translit: ādityānām ahaṁ viṣhṇur jyotiṣhāṁ ravir anśhumān marīchir marutām asmi nakṣhatrāṇām ahaṁ śhaśhī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Of the Vedas I am the Sâma-Veda; I am Vâsava of the Shining Ones; and of the senses I am the mind; I am of living beings the intelligence.
_nearmiss_src.original: वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः। इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: vedānāṁ sāma-vedo ’smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ indriyāṇāṁ manaśh chāsmi bhūtānām asmi chetanā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And of the Rudras Shañkara am I; Vittesha of the Yakshas and Râkshasas; and of the Vasus I am Pâvaka; Meru of high mountains am I.
_nearmiss_src.original: रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्। वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: rudrāṇāṁ śhaṅkaraśh chāsmi vitteśho yakṣha-rakṣhasām vasūnāṁ pāvakaśh chāsmi meruḥ śhikhariṇām aham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And know Me, O Pârtha, of household priests the chief, Brihaspati: of generals I am Skanda; of lakes I am the ocean.
_nearmiss_src.original: पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्। सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: purodhasāṁ cha mukhyaṁ māṁ viddhi pārtha bṛihaspatim senānīnām ahaṁ skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ

gold_answer: 1.29 — Arjuna's limbs fail, mouth dries, body trembles, hair stands on end.
question: Which verse describes Arjuna's limbs failing, his mouth parching, his body quivering, and his hair standing on end?

hi.gold_answer: 29-अर्जुन के अंग विफल हो जाते हैं, मुँह सूख जाता है, शरीर कांप जाता है, बाल अंत में खड़े हो जाते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक अर्जुन के अंगों के टूटने, उनके मुंह के सूखने, उनके शरीर के कांपने और उनके बालों के अंत में खड़े होने का वर्णन करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 1. 29-अर्जुनस्य अङ्गानि विफलाः भवन्ति, मुखः शुष्कः भवति, शरीरम् कम्पते, केशः अन्ते स्थिताः भवन्ति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके अर्जुनस्य अङ्गानां विफलतायाः, तस्य मुखस्य दाहस्य, तस्य शरीरस्य कम्पनस्य, अन्ते स्थितस्य केशस्य च वर्णनं कृतम् अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: My limbs fail and my mouth is parched, my body quivers, and my hair stands on end,
_gold_src.original: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति। वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
_gold_src.translit: sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Deeply moved to pity, thus uttered in sadness: Arjuna said: Seeing these my kinsmen, O Krishna, arrayed, eager to fight,
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच कृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत्। दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Gândîva slips from my hand, and my skin burns all over, I am not able to stand, my mind is whirling,
_nearmiss_src.original: गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते। न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: gāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyate na cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sanjaya said: Having thus spoken on the battle-field, Arjuna sank down on the seat of the chariot, casting away his bow and arrow, his mind overborne by grief.
_nearmiss_src.original: सञ्जय उवाच एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्। विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sañjaya uvācha evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśhat visṛijya sa-śharaṁ chāpaṁ śhoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ

gold_answer: 2.69 — the sage is awake in what is night to others, and is unmoved by what stirs them.
question: Which verse says that what is night for all beings is the time of waking for the disciplined one, and vice versa?

hi.gold_answer: 69-ऋषि दूसरों के लिए रात में जागते हैं, और जो उन्हें हिलाता है उससे विचलित नहीं होते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि सभी प्राणियों के लिए जो रात है वह अनुशासित के लिए जागने का समय है, और इसके विपरीत?

sa.gold_answer: 2. 69-अन्येभ्यः रात्रौ ऋषिः जागरति, तान् उद्वेलयति चेत् सः अचञ्चलः भवति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः वदति यत् सर्वेषां प्राणिनां कृते रात्रिः कः अनुशासितस्य कृते जागरणसमयः इति, तथा च तद्विरुद्धम् इति?

_gold_src.text_en: That which is the night of all beings, for the disciplined man is the time of waking; when other beings are waking, then is it night for the sage who seeth.
_gold_src.original: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी। यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
_gold_src.translit: yā niśhā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti sanyamī yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśhā paśhyato muneḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Therefore, O mighty-armed, whose senses are all completely restrained from the objects of sense, of him the understanding is well poised.
_nearmiss_src.original: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛihītāni sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He attaineth Peace, into whom all desires flow as rivers flow into the ocean, which is filled with water, but remaineth unmoved—not he who desireth desires.
_nearmiss_src.original: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्। तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: āpūryamāṇam achala-pratiṣhṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśhanti yadvat tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśhanti sarve sa śhāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whoso forsaketh all desires and goeth onwards free from yearnings, selfless and without egoism—he goeth to Peace.
_nearmiss_src.original: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः। निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumānśh charati niḥspṛihaḥ nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sa śhāntim adhigachchhati

gold_answer: 6.34 — Arjuna calls the mind restless and as hard to control as the wind.
question: In which verse does Arjuna compare the restless mind to the wind and call it as hard to curb?

hi.gold_answer: 6. 34-अर्जुन मन को बेचैन और हवा की तरह नियंत्रित करना कठिन कहता है।
hi.question: अर्जुन किस श्लोक में बेचैन मन की तुलना हवा से करते हैं और इसे नियंत्रित करना कठिन कहते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 6. 34-अर्जुनः मनम् अस्थिरं, वायोः सदृशं नियन्त्रणाय कठिनं च इति वदति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके अर्जुनः चञ्चलमनस्य वायुना सह तुलनां कृत्वा तं निग्रहयितुं कठिनम् इति वदति?

_gold_src.text_en: For the mind is verily restless, O Krishna; it is impetuous, strong and difficult to bend. I deem it as hard to curb as the wind.
_gold_src.original: चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम्। तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: chañchalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛiṣhṇa pramāthi balavad dṛiḍham tasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva su-duṣhkaram

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Without doubt, O mighty-armed, the mind is hard to curb and restless; but it may be curbed by constant practice and by dispassion.
_nearmiss_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलं। अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhrī bhagavān uvācha asanśhayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ chalam abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛihyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: This yoga which Thou hast declared to be by equanimity, O Madhusûdana, I see not a stable foundation for it, owing to restlessness;
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन। एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात् स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana etasyāhaṁ na paśhyāmi chañchalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As often as the wavering and unsteady mind goeth forth, so often reining it in, let him bring it under the control of the Self .
_nearmiss_src.original: यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्। ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśhaṁ nayet

gold_answer: 8.17 — a day of Brahma lasts a thousand ages, and the night as long.
question: Which verse measures a day of Brahma as a thousand ages and the night likewise a thousand ages?

hi.gold_answer: 8. 17-ब्रह्मा का एक दिन एक हजार युग तक चलता है, और रात उतनी ही लंबी होती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक ब्रह्मा के एक दिन को एक हजार युग और रात को इसी तरह एक हजार युग के रूप में मापता है?

sa.gold_answer: 8. 17-ब्रह्मणः एकः दिवसः सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तं, रात्रिः च दीर्घकालं यावत् भवति।
sa.question: कस्मिन् श्लोके ब्रह्मस्य एकं दिनम् सहस्रयुगेण, रात्रिः अपि सहस्रयुगेण इति परिमाप्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: The people who know the day of Brahmâ, a thousand ages in duration, and the night a thousand ages in ending, they know day and night.
_gold_src.original: सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः। रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sahasra-yuga-paryantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ rātriṁ yuga-sahasrāntāṁ te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: From the unmanifested all the manifested stream forth at the coming of day; at the coming of night they dissolve, even in That called the unmanifested.
_nearmiss_src.original: अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे। रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ prabhavantyahar-āgame rātryāgame pralīyante tatraivāvyakta-sanjñake

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The worlds, beginning with the world of Brahmâ, they come and go, O Arjuna; but he who cometh unto Me, O Kaunteya, he knoweth birth no more.
_nearmiss_src.original: आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन। मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ā-brahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ punar āvartino ’rjuna mām upetya tu kaunteya punar janma na vidyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: This multitude of beings, going forth repeatedly, is dissolved at the coming of night: by ordination, O Pârtha, it streams forth at the coming of day.
_nearmiss_src.original: भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते। रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṁ bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate rātryāgame ’vaśhaḥ pārtha prabhavatyahar-āgame

gold_answer: 7.8 — 'I am the sapidity in waters, the radiance in moon and sun.'
question: Which verse declares the Lord to be the taste in water and the light in the moon and the sun?

hi.gold_answer: 7. 8 -'मैं पानी में तेज हूँ, चंद्रमा और सूर्य में चमक।'
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक भगवान को पानी में स्वाद और चंद्रमा और सूर्य में प्रकाश घोषित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 7. 8-"अहं जलेषु तेजः, चन्द्रस्य सूर्यस्य च तेजः" इति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः भगवन्तं जलस्य स्वादः, चन्द्रस्य सूर्यस्य च प्रकाशः इति घोषयति?

_gold_src.text_en: I the sapidity in waters, O son of Kuntî, I the radiance in moon and sun; the Word of Power in all the Vedas, sound in ether, and virility in men;
_gold_src.original: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः। प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥
_gold_src.translit: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śhaśhi-sūryayoḥ praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣhu śhabdaḥ khe pauruṣhaṁ nṛiṣhu

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The pure fragrance of earths and the bril liance in fire am I; the life in all beings am I, and the austerity in ascetics.
_nearmiss_src.original: पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ। जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛithivyāṁ cha tejaśh chāsmi vibhāvasau jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu tapaśh chāsmi tapasviṣhu

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Know Me, O Pârtha! as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the Reason of the Reason-endowed, the splendour of splendid things am I.
_nearmiss_src.original: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्। बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There is naught whatsoever higher than I, O Dhananjaya. All this is threaded on Me, as rows of pearls on a string.
_nearmiss_src.original: मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय। मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjaya mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva

gold_answer: 9.32 — all who take refuge in the Lord, regardless of birth, tread the highest path.
question: Which verse affirms that even those of lowly birth or station who take refuge in the Lord reach the highest goal?

hi.gold_answer: 9: 32-जो कोई भी भगवान की शरण लेता है, जन्म की परवाह किए बिना, उच्चतम मार्ग पर चलता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक इस बात की पुष्टि करता है कि निम्न जन्म या स्थान के लोग भी जो भगवान की शरण लेते हैं, वे उच्चतम लक्ष्य तक पहुँचते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: 9: 32 यः कश्चित् भगवन्तं शरणम् अगृहति, जन्मतः सर्व्वे उत्तमा मार्गेण गच्छन्ति।
sa.question: निम्नजन्मयुक्ताः अथवा स्थिताः ये भगवन्तं शरणम् अगच्छन्ति ते अपि सर्वोच्चं लक्ष्यं प्राप्तवन्तः इति कः श्लोकः पुष्टिं करोति?
