_gold_src.text_en: They who take refuge with Me, O Partha, though of the womb of sin, women, Vaishyas, even Shûdras, they also tread the highest path.
_gold_src.original: मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः। स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: māṁ hi pārtha vyapāśhritya ye ’pi syuḥ pāpa-yonayaḥ striyo vaiśhyās tathā śhūdrās te ’pi yānti parāṁ gatim

_nearmiss_src.text_en: How much rather then holy Brâhmanas and devoted royal saints; having obtained this transient joyless world, worship thou Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा। अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kiṁ punar brāhmaṇāḥ puṇyā bhaktā rājarṣhayas tathā anityam asukhaṁ lokam imaṁ prāpya bhajasva mām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Even if the most sinful worship Me, with un divided heart, he too must be accounted righteous, for he hath rightly resolved;
_nearmiss_src.original: अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक्। साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: api chet su-durāchāro bhajate mām ananya-bhāk sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Speedily he becometh dutiful and goeth to eternal peace; O Kaunteya, know thou for certain that My devotee perisheth never.
_nearmiss_src.original: क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति। कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kṣhipraṁ bhavati dharmātmā śhaśhvach-chhāntiṁ nigachchhati kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśhyati

gold_answer: 15.7 — an eternal portion of the Lord becomes the soul, drawing the mind and senses.
question: Which verse describes the individual soul as an eternal fragment of the Lord that draws to itself the mind and the senses?

hi.gold_answer: 15. 7-भगवान का एक शाश्वत हिस्सा आत्मा बन जाता है, जो मन और इंद्रियों को आकर्षित करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक व्यक्तिगत आत्मा को भगवान के एक शाश्वत टुकड़े के रूप में वर्णित करता है जो मन और इंद्रियों को अपनी ओर आकर्षित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 15. 7-भगवतः शाश्वतः अंशः प्राणः भवति, मनसि इन्द्रियान् च आकर्षयति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः व्यक्तिगतात्मं भगवतः अनन्तः खण्डः इति वर्णयति यः मनसि इन्द्रियां च आकर्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: A portion of Mine own Self, transformed in the world of life into an immortal Spirit, draweth round itself the senses of which the mind is the sixth, veiled in matter.
_gold_src.original: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥
_gold_src.translit: mamaivānśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ manaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: When the Lord acquireth a body and when He abandoneth it, He seizeth these and goeth with them, as the wind takes fragrances from their retreats
_nearmiss_src.original: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः।गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śharīraṁ yad avāpnoti yach chāpy utkrāmatīśhvaraḥ gṛihītvaitāni sanyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśhayāt

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Enshrined in the ear, the eye, the touch, the taste and the smell, and in the mind also, He enjoyeth the objects of the senses
_nearmiss_src.original: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च।अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhrotraṁ chakṣhuḥ sparśhanaṁ cha rasanaṁ ghrāṇam eva cha adhiṣhṭhāya manaśh chāyaṁ viṣhayān upasevate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There are two Energies in this world, the destructible and the indestructible; the destructible is all beings, the unchanging is called the indestructible.
_nearmiss_src.original: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च।क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: dvāv imau puruṣhau loke kṣharaśh chākṣhara eva cha kṣharaḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭa-stho ’kṣhara uchyate

gold_answer: 13.8 — wisdom begins with humility, unpretentiousness, harmlessness, forgiveness, and uprightness.
question: Which verse begins the enumeration of wisdom with humility, unpretentiousness, and harmlessness?

hi.gold_answer: 13. 8-ज्ञान की शुरुआत विनम्रता, सरलता, हानिरहितता, क्षमा और ईमानदारी से होती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक विनम्रता, सरलता और हानिरहितता के साथ ज्ञान की गणना शुरू करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 13. 8-ज्ञानं विनम्रतया, निष्कपटतया, हानिरहिततया, क्षमायां, सत्येन च आरभ्यते।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः विनम्रतया, निस्सहायतया, हानिरहिततया च ज्ञानस्य गणनां आरभते?

_gold_src.text_en: Humility, unpretentiousness, harmlessness, forgiveness, rectitude, service of the teacher, purity, steadfastness, self-control.
_gold_src.original: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥
_gold_src.translit: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavam āchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Dispassion towards the objects of the senses, and also absence of egoism, insight into the pain and evil of birth, death, old age and sickness,
_nearmiss_src.original: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: indriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva cha janma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Unattachment, absence of self-identification with son, wife or home, and constant balance of mind in wished-for and unwished-for events,
_nearmiss_src.original: असक्ितरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: asaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhu nityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhu

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, combination, intelligence, firmness; these, briefly described, constitute the Field and its modifications.
_nearmiss_src.original: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः।एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ichchhā dveṣhaḥ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṅghātaśh chetanā dhṛitiḥ etat kṣhetraṁ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛitam

gold_answer: 17.20 — a sattvic gift is given as a duty, in fit place and time, to a worthy person, expecting nothing.
question: Which verse defines the pure gift as one given without thought of return, in a fit place and time, to a worthy person?

hi.gold_answer: 17.20-एक योग्य व्यक्ति को एक कर्तव्य के रूप में, उपयुक्त स्थान और समय पर, एक योग्य व्यक्ति को दिया जाता है, जो कुछ भी उम्मीद नहीं करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक शुद्ध उपहार को एक योग्य व्यक्ति को वापस करने के बारे में सोचे बिना, एक उपयुक्त स्थान और समय पर दिए जाने के रूप में परिभाषित करता है?

sa.gold_answer: 17.20-किमपि न अपेक्षमाणस्य योग्यव्यक्तेः कृते कर्तव्यरूपेण, उचितस्थाने समये च, सात्त्विकं उपहारं दत्तं भवति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः शुद्धं उपहारं निर्वचयति यत् योग्यव्यक्तेः कृते प्रतिदानस्य विचारं विना, उचितस्थाने समये च दत्तम् इति?

_gold_src.text_en: That alms given to one who does nothing in return, believing that a gift ought to be made in a fit place and time to a worthy person, that alms is accounted pure.
_gold_src.original: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे।देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ dīyate ‘nupakāriṇe deśhe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānaṁ sāttvikaṁ smṛitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That given with a view to receiving in return, or looking for fruit again, or grudgingly, that alms is accounted of passion.
_nearmiss_src.original: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः।दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yat tu pratyupakārārthaṁ phalam uddiśhya vā punaḥ dīyate cha parikliṣhṭaṁ tad dānaṁ rājasaṁ smṛitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That alms given at unfit place and time, and to unworthy persons, disrespectfully and contemptuously, that is declared of darkness.
_nearmiss_src.original: अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते।असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: adeśha-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaśh cha dīyate asat-kṛitam avajñātaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Worship given to the Gods, to the twice-born, to the teachers and to the wise, purity, straightforwardness, continence and harmlessness, are called the austerity of the body.
_nearmiss_src.original: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्।ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: deva-dwija-guru-prājña- pūjanaṁ śhaucham ārjavam brahmacharyam ahinsā cha śhārīraṁ tapa uchyate

gold_answer: 18.61 — the Lord in the heart whirls all beings by his illusive power, as on a potter's wheel.
question: Which verse says the Lord dwells in the heart of all beings, whirling them by his power as though they were mounted on a machine?

hi.gold_answer: 18.61-हृदय में भगवान सभी प्राणियों को अपनी भ्रामक शक्ति से घुमाते हैं, जैसे कुम्हार के चक्र पर।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि भगवान सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में निवास करते हैं, उन्हें अपनी शक्ति से घुमाते हैं जैसे कि वे एक मशीन पर सवार थे?

sa.gold_answer: 18.61-हृदयस्थः प्रभुः कुम्भकारस्य चक्रस्य इव स्वस्य भ्रमात्मकशक्त्या सर्वान् जीवान् परिभ्रमति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः कथयति यत् भगवतः सर्वप्राणिनां हृदये निवसति, यन्त्रस्य उपरि स्थापितवत् स्वशक्त्या तान् परिभ्रमति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Lord dwelleth in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, by His illusive Power causing all beings to revolve, as though mounted on a potter's wheel.
_gold_src.original: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति।भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥
_gold_src.translit: īśhvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ hṛid-deśhe ‘rjuna tiṣhṭhati bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Flee unto Him for shelter with all thy being, O Bhârata; by His grace thou shalt obtain supreme peace, the everlasting dwelling-place.
_nearmiss_src.original: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tam eva śharaṇaṁ gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata tat-prasādāt parāṁ śhāntiṁ sthānaṁ prāpsyasi śhāśhvatam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: O son of Kuntî, bound by thine own duty born of thine own nature, that which from delusion thou desirest not to do, even that helplessly thou shalt perform.
_nearmiss_src.original: स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा।कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: swbhāva-jena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā kartuṁ nechchhasi yan mohāt kariṣhyasy avaśho ’pi tat

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And I am seated in the hearts of all, and from Me memory and wisdom and their absence. And that which is to be known in all the Vedas am I; and I indeed the Veda-knower and the author of the Vedanta.
_nearmiss_src.original: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sarvasya chāhaṁ hṛidi sanniviṣhṭo mattaḥ smṛitir jñānam apohanaṁ cha vedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛid veda-vid eva chāham

gold_answer: Act for the action's sake, never for its fruit (2.47), performing duty in union and balanced in success and failure — that equanimity is yoga (2.48).
question: What does the Gita teach about performing one's duty while remaining detached from the results of action?

hi.gold_answer: कर्म के लिए कार्य करें, कभी भी उसके फल के लिए नहीं (2.47), संघ में कर्तव्य का पालन करना और सफलता और विफलता में संतुलित होना-कि समता ही योग है (2.48)।
hi.question: गीता कर्म के परिणामों से अलग रहते हुए अपने कर्तव्य का पालन करने के बारे में क्या सिखाती है?

sa.gold_answer: कर्मार्थं कार्यं करोतु, कदापि तस्य फलाय न करोतु (2.47), संयोगेन कर्तव्यं निर्वहतु, सफलतायां विफलतायां च सन्तुलितं करोतु-समता एव योगः (2.48)।
sa.question: कर्मफलात् विमुखः भूत्वा कर्तव्यपालनस्य विषये गीता किं शिक्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Thy business is with the action only, never with its fruits; so let not the fruit of action be thy motive, nor be thou to inaction attached.
_gold_src.original: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
_gold_src.translit: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi

_gold_src.text_en: Perform action, O Dhananjaya, dwelling in union with the divine, renouncing attachments and balanced evenly in success and failure: equilibrium is called yoga.
_gold_src.original: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय। सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
_gold_src.translit: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Sages, united to the Pure Reason, renounce the fruit which action yieldeth, and, liberated from the bonds of birth, they go to the blissful seat.
_nearmiss_src.original: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः। जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥ janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty-anāmayam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Surrendering all actions to Me, with thy thoughts resting on the supreme Self , from hope and egoism freed, and of mental fever cured, engage in battle.
_nearmiss_src.original: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा। निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ

gold_answer: Brooding on objects breeds attachment, then desire, then anger (2.62); anger breeds delusion, lost memory, ruined reason, and destruction (2.63).
question: How does the Gita describe the psychological progression that begins with dwelling on sense-objects and ends in a person's ruin?

hi.gold_answer: वस्तुओं पर झुकने से आसक्ति पैदा होती है, फिर इच्छा, फिर क्रोध (2.62); क्रोध से भ्रम पैदा होता है, स्मृति खो जाती है, बुद्धि खराब हो जाती है और विनाश होता है (2.63)।
hi.question: गीता उस मनोवैज्ञानिक प्रगति का वर्णन कैसे करती है जो इन्द्रिय-वस्तुओं पर ध्यान देने से शुरू होती है और किसी व्यक्ति के विनाश में समाप्त होती है?

sa.gold_answer: वस्तूनां उपरि प्रवृत्ता आसक्तिः, ततः कामना, ततः क्रोधः (2.62) जनयति; क्रोधः भ्रमं जनयति, स्मृतिक्षयः जनयति, बुद्धिभ्रान्तिः जनयति, विनाशः जनयति (2.63)।
sa.question: गीता कथं मनोवैज्ञानिक-प्रगतिं वर्णयति यत् इन्द्रिय-वस्तूनां ध्यानात् आरभ्य व्यक्तेः विनाशे समाप्यते?

_gold_src.text_en: Man, musing on the objects of sense, conceiveth an attachment to these; from attachment ariseth desire; from desire anger cometh forth;
_gold_src.original: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते। सङ्गात् संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
_gold_src.translit: dhyāyato viṣhayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣhūpajāyate saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate

_gold_src.text_en: From anger proceedeth delusion; from delusion confused memory; from confused memory the destruction of Reason; from destruction of Reason he perishes.
_gold_src.original: क्रोधाद्भवति संमोहः संमोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः। स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
_gold_src.translit: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛiti-vibhramaḥ smṛiti-bhranśhād buddhi-nāśho buddhi-nāśhāt praṇaśhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: It is desire, it is wrath, begotten by the quality of motion; all-consuming, all-polluting, know thou this as our foe here on earth.
_nearmiss_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः। महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhrī bhagavān uvācha kāma eṣha krodha eṣha rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ mahāśhano mahā-pāpmā viddhyenam iha vairiṇam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But the disciplined self, moving among sense-objects with senses free from attraction and repulsion, mastered by the Self, goeth to peace.
_nearmiss_src.original: रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन्। आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: rāga-dveṣha-viyuktais tu viṣhayān indriyaiśh charan ātma-vaśhyair-vidheyātmā prasādam adhigachchhati

gold_answer: The Self is unborn and undying (2.20); weapons, fire, water, and wind cannot harm it (2.23); it is uncleavable, all-pervading, and eternal (2.24).
question: How does the Gita argue that the true self cannot be destroyed?

hi.gold_answer: आत्मा अजन्मा और अमर है (2.20); हथियार, आग, पानी और हवा इसे नुकसान नहीं पहुँचा सकते (2.23); यह अशुद्ध, सर्वव्यापी और शाश्वत है (2.24)।
hi.question: गीता कैसे तर्क देती है कि सच्चे आत्मा को नष्ट नहीं किया जा सकता है?

sa.gold_answer: आत्मा अजन्मा च अमरः अस्ति (2.20); शस्त्राणि, अग्निः, जलं, वायुः च तस्य हानिं कर्तुं न शक्नुवन्ति (2.23); सः अशुद्धः, सर्वव्यापि, नित्यः च अस्ति (2.24)।
sa.question: गीता कथं तर्कयति यत् सत्यात्मः विनाशयितुं न शक्नोति इति?

_gold_src.text_en: He is not born, nor doth he die; nor having been, ceaseth he any more to be; unborn, perpetual, eternal and ancient, he is not slain when the body is slaughtered.
_gold_src.original: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
_gold_src.translit: na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre

_gold_src.text_en: Weapons cleave him not, nor fire burneth him, nor waters wet him, nor wind drieth him away.
_gold_src.original: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः। न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
_gold_src.translit: nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ na chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Uncleavable he, incombustible he, and indeed neither to be wetted nor dried away; perpetual, all-pervasive, stable, immovable, ancient.
_gold_src.original: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च। नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śhoṣhya eva cha nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As a man, casting off worn-out garments, taketh new ones, so the dweller in the body, casting off worn-out bodies, entereth into others that are new.
_nearmiss_src.original: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि। तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇi tathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānya nyāni sanyāti navāni dehī

_nearmiss_src.text_en: This dweller in the body of everyone is ever invulnerable, O Bhârata; therefore thou shouldst not grieve for any creature.
_nearmiss_src.original: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत। तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi

gold_answer: Whenever righteousness declines and unrighteousness rises (4.7), the Lord is born age after age to protect the good, destroy evil, and restore dharma (4.8).
question: What reason does the Gita give for the Lord's repeated incarnation in the world?

hi.gold_answer: जब भी धार्मिकता कम होती है और अधार्मिकता बढ़ती है (4.7), भगवान अच्छे की रक्षा करने, बुराई को नष्ट करने और धर्म को बहाल करने के लिए उम्र के बाद उम्र में पैदा होते हैं (4.8)।
hi.question: संसार में भगवान के बार-बार अवतार के लिए गीता क्या कारण देती है?

sa.gold_answer: यदा धार्म्मिकतायाः क्षयः भवति तथा च अनीतिः वर्धते (4.7) तदा भगवतः उत्तमानां रक्षणार्थं, दुष्टानां विनाशनार्थं, धर्मस्य पुनःस्थापनार्थं च युगानुयुगे जनयति (4.8)।
sa.question: जगति भगवतः अवतारस्य पुनरावर्तनाय गीता किं कारणं ददति?

_gold_src.text_en: Whenever there is decay of righteousness, O Bhârata, and there is exaltation of unrighteousness, then I Myself come forth;
_gold_src.original: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛijāmyaham

_gold_src.text_en: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of evil-doers, for the sake of firmly establishing righteousness, I am born from age to age.
_gold_src.original: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे ॥
_gold_src.translit: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām dharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Though unborn, the imperishable Self , and also the Lord of all beings, brooding over nature which is Mine own, yet I am born through My own Power.
_nearmiss_src.original: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्। प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi san prakṛitiṁ svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who thus knoweth My divine birth and action, in its essence, having abandoned the body, cometh not to birth again, but cometh unto Me, O Arjuna.
_nearmiss_src.original: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः। त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: janma karma cha me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

gold_answer: Sattva binds by attachment to happiness and knowledge (14.6), rajas by attachment to action and craving (14.7), and tamas by heedlessness, indolence, and sleep (14.8).
question: How does the Gita describe the way the three qualities of nature bind the embodied soul?

hi.gold_answer: सत्व सुख और ज्ञान के प्रति लगाव (14.6), राज कर्म और लालसा के प्रति लगाव (14.7), और तामस लापरवाही, आलस्य और नींद (14.8) से जुड़ता है।
hi.question: जिस तरह से प्रकृति के तीन गुण आत्मा को बांधते हैं, गीता उसका वर्णन कैसे करती है?

sa.gold_answer: सत्वः सुखेन ज्ञानेन च आसक्तिं करोति (14.6), रजः क्रियया तृष्णया च आसक्तिं करोति (14.7), तमः अवधानेन, आलस्येन, निद्रया च बद्धः भवति (14.8)।
sa.question: प्रकृत्याः त्रीणि गुणानि मूर्तात्मं कथं बन्धयन्ति इति गीता कथं वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Of these Harmony from its stainlessness, luminous and healthy, bindeth by the attachment to bliss and the attachment to wisdom, O sinless one.
_gold_src.original: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्।सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥
_gold_src.translit: tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśhakam anāmayam sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha

_gold_src.text_en: Motion, the passion nature, know thou, is the source of attachment and thirst for life, O Kaunteya, that bindeth the dweller in the body by the attachment to action.
_gold_src.original: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्।तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛiṣhṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam

_gold_src.text_en: But Inertia, know thou, born of unwisdom, is the deluder of all dwellers in the body; that bindeth by heedlessness, indolence and sloth, O Bharata .
_gold_src.original: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्।प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥
_gold_src.translit: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Harmony, Motion, Inertia, such are the qualities, Matter-born; they bind fast in the body, O great-armed one, the indestructible dweller in the body.
_nearmiss_src.original: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः।निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛiti-sambhavāḥ nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Harmony attacheth to bliss, Motion to action O Bharata . Inertia, verily, having shrouded wisdom, attacheth on the contrary to heedlessness.
_nearmiss_src.original: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत।ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata jñānam āvṛitya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta

gold_answer: The Lord accepts even a leaf, flower, fruit, or water given with devotion (9.26); he is the same to all beings, yet present in those who worship him with love (9.29).
question: What does the Gita teach about the simplicity of devotional offering and the Lord's impartiality toward all devotees?

hi.gold_answer: भगवान भक्ति के साथ दिए गए एक पत्ते, फूल, फल या पानी को भी स्वीकार करते हैं (9.26); वह सभी प्राणियों के लिए समान हैं, फिर भी प्यार से उनकी पूजा करने वालों में मौजूद हैं (9.29)।
hi.question: भक्ति समर्पण की सरलता और सभी भक्तों के प्रति भगवान की निष्पक्षता के बारे में गीता क्या सिखाती है?

sa.gold_answer: भगवतः भक्त्या दत्तं पत्रं, पुष्पं, फलं, जलं वा अपि स्वीकरोति (9.26); सः सर्वप्राणिनां कृते समानः अस्ति, तथापि ये प्रेमेन तस्य पूजां कुर्वन्ति तेषु उपस्थितः अस्ति (9.29)।
sa.question: भक्ति-समर्पणस्य सरलतायाः विषये, सर्वे भक्तानां प्रति भगवतः निष्पक्षतायाः विषये च गीता किं शिक्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: He who offereth to Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, water, that I accept from the striving self, offered as it is with devotion.
_gold_src.original: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति। तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: patraṁ puṣhpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati tadahaṁ bhaktyupahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: The same am I to all beings; there is none hateful to Me nor dear. They verily who worship Me with devotion, they are in Me, and I also in them.
_gold_src.original: समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः। ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: samo ’haṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo ’sti na priyaḥ ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpyaham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whatsoever thou doest, whatsoever thou eatest, whatsoever thou offerest, whatsoever thou givest, whatsoever thou doest of austerity, O Kaunteya, do thou that as an offering unto Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्। यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yat karoṣhi yad aśhnāsi yaj juhoṣhi dadāsi yat yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣhva mad-arpaṇam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: On Me fix thy mind, be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; prostrate thyself before Me; harmonised thus in the Self , thou shalt come unto Me, having Me as thy supreme goal.
_nearmiss_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु। मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ

gold_answer: Such a one abandons all cravings and is content in the Self (2.55), is unshaken in pain and pleasure and free of fear and anger (2.56), and is unattached, neither rejoicing nor recoiling at fortune (2.57).
question: How does the Gita characterize the person of steady wisdom (sthitaprajna)?

hi.gold_answer: ऐसा व्यक्ति सभी इच्छाओं को छोड़ देता है और आत्मा में संतुष्ट रहता है (2.55), दर्द और सुख में अडिग रहता है और भय और क्रोध से मुक्त रहता है (2.56), और असंबद्ध रहता है, न तो आनंद करता है और न ही भाग्य में पश्चाताप करता है (2.57)।
hi.question: गीता स्थिर ज्ञान (स्थिरप्रज्ञा) वाले व्यक्ति की विशेषता कैसे दर्शाती है?

sa.gold_answer: एतादृशः सर्वान् तृष्णान् परित्यज्य आत्मानं सन्तुष्टः भवति (2.55), दुःखेन सुखेन च अविचलः, भयेन क्रोधेन च मुक्तः (2.56), असम्बद्धः, न आनन्देन, न भाग्यानाम् अनुतापेन (2.57)।
sa.question: गीता कथं स्थिरज्ञानस्य (स्थिरप्रज्ञायाः) व्यक्तेः लक्षणं करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: When a man abandoneth, O Pârtha, all the desires of the heart, and is satisfied in the Self by the Self , then is he called stable in mind.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान्। आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī bhagavān uvācha prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān ātmany-evātmanā tuṣhṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate

_gold_src.text_en: He whose mind is free from anxiety amid pains, indifferent amid pleasures, loosed from passion, fear and anger, is called a sage of stable mind.
_gold_src.original: दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः। वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥
_gold_src.translit: duḥkheṣhv-anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣhu vigata-spṛihaḥ vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir uchyate

_gold_src.text_en: He who on every side is without attachments, whatever hap of fair and foul, who neither likes nor dislikes, of such a one the understanding is well poised.
_gold_src.original: यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्। नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_gold_src.translit: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śhubhāśhubham nābhinandati na dveṣhṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: When, again, as a tortoise draws in on all sides its limbs, he withdraws his senses from the objects of sense, then is his understanding well poised.
_nearmiss_src.original: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yadā sanharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Arjuna said: What the mark, of him who is stable of mind, steadfast in contemplation, O Keshava? How doth the stable-minded talk, how doth he sit, how walk?
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव। स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣhā samādhi-sthasya keśhava sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣheta kim āsīta vrajeta kim

gold_answer: By the path of fire, light, and the northern course, the knowers of Brahman go and return not (8.24); by smoke, night, and the southern course, the yogi returns (8.25); these bright and dark paths are the world's enduring courses (8.26).
question: How does the Gita describe the two paths by which departing souls travel, of light and of darkness?

hi.gold_answer: अग्नि, प्रकाश और उत्तरी मार्ग से ब्रह्म के ज्ञाता जाते हैं और वापस नहीं आते हैं (8.24); धुएँ, रात और दक्षिणी मार्ग से योगी लौटते हैं (8.25); ये उज्ज्वल और काले मार्ग दुनिया के स्थायी मार्ग हैं (8.26)।
hi.question: गीता उन दो मार्गों का वर्णन कैसे करती है जिनसे आत्माएँ यात्रा करती हैं, प्रकाश और अंधेरा?

sa.gold_answer: अग्न्यः, प्रकाशस्य, उत्तरमार्गस्य च मार्गेण ब्रह्मज्ञाः गच्छन्ति, न प्रत्यागमन्ति (8.24); धूमद्वारा, रात्र्या, दक्षिणमार्गेण च योगी पुनरागमनं करोति (8.25); एते उज्ज्वलाः अन्धकाराः च मार्गाः विश्वस्य चिरस्थायिः मार्गाः सन्ति (8.26)।
sa.question: गीता कथं प्रकाशस्य अन्धकारस्य च मार्गद्वयम् वर्णयति यया निर्गमनात्माः यात्रां कुर्वन्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: Fire, light, day-time, the bright fortnight, the six months of the northern path—then, going forth, the men who know the Eternal go to the Eternal .
_gold_src.original: अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम्। तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥
_gold_src.translit: agnir jyotir ahaḥ śhuklaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam tatra prayātā gachchhanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Smoke, night-time, the dark fortnight also, the six months of the southern path—then the Yogî, obtaining the moonlight, returneth.
_gold_src.original: धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्। तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ॥
_gold_src.translit: dhūmo rātris tathā kṛiṣhṇaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā dakṣhiṇāyanam tatra chāndramasaṁ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate

_gold_src.text_en: Light and darkness, these are thought to be the world's everlasting paths; by the one he goeth who returneth not, by the other he who returneth again.
_gold_src.original: शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते। एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययाऽऽवर्तते पुनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhukla-kṛiṣhṇe gatī hyete jagataḥ śhāśhvate mate ekayā yātyanāvṛittim anyayāvartate punaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: That time wherein going forth Yogîs return not, and also that wherein going forth they return, that time shall I declare to thee, O prince of the Bhâratas.
_nearmiss_src.original: यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः। प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yatra kāle tvanāvṛittim āvṛittiṁ chaiva yoginaḥ prayātā yānti taṁ kālaṁ vakṣhyāmi bharatarṣhabha

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Knowing these paths, O Pârtha, the Yogi is nowise perplexed. Therefore in all times be firm in yoga, O Arjuna.
_nearmiss_src.original: नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन। तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: naite sṛitī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaśhchana tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu yoga-yukto bhavārjuna

gold_answer: The divinely-born have fearlessness, purity, self-restraint, harmlessness, truth, and gentleness (16.1-16.2), while the demoniac are marked by hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, wrath, harshness, and ignorance (16.4).
question: How does the Gita contrast those born with a divine nature against those born with a demoniac nature?

hi.gold_answer: ईश्वरीय रूप से जन्मे लोगों में निर्भीकता, शुद्धता, आत्म-संयम, हानिरहितता, सत्य और कोमलता (16.1-16.2) होती है, जबकि राक्षसी लोगों में पाखंड, अहंकार, घमंड, क्रोध, कठोरता और अज्ञानता होती है (16.4)।
hi.question: गीता दिव्य प्रकृति के साथ पैदा होने वालों की तुलना राक्षसी प्रकृति के साथ पैदा होने वालों से कैसे करती है?

sa.gold_answer: दैवीजनेषु निर्भयता, पवित्रता, आत्मनियन्त्रणं, हानिरहितता, सत्यं, सौम्यता (16.1-16.2) च भवति, यदा तु आसुरीजनेषु कपटम्, अहंकारः, अहंकारः, क्रोधः, कठोरता, अज्ञानः च लक्षिताः भवन्ति (16.4)।
sa.question: देवप्रकृत्या जन्मितानां, आसुरीप्रकृत्या जन्मितानां च मध्ये गीता कथं वैषम्यम् प्रकटयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, cleanness of life, steadfastness in the Yoga of wisdom, almsgiving, self-restraint and sacrifice and study of the Scriptures, austerity and straightforwardness,
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः। दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha abhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ dānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam

_gold_src.text_en: Harmlessness, truth, absence of wrath, renunciation, peacefulness, absence of crookedness, compassion to living beings, uncovetousness, mildness, modesty, absence of fickleness,
_gold_src.original: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम्।दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: ahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunam dayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalam

_gold_src.text_en: Hypocrisy, arrogance and conceit, wrath and also harshness and unwisdom are his who is born, O Pârtha, with demoniacal properties.
_gold_src.original: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च।अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: dambho darpo ’bhimānaśh cha krodhaḥ pāruṣhyam eva cha ajñānaṁ chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Vigour, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of envy and pride—these are his who is born with the divine properties, O Bhârata.
_nearmiss_src.original: तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता। भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitā bhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The divine properties are deemed to be for liberation, the demoniacal for bondage. Grieve not, thou art born with divine properties, O Pândava.
_nearmiss_src.original: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता।मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: daivī sampad vimokṣhāya nibandhāyāsurī matā mā śhuchaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava

gold_answer: One who hates no being, is friendly, compassionate, free of egoism, balanced in pleasure and pain, forgiving, ever content, self-controlled, and devoted in mind and reason is dear to the Lord (12.13-12.14).
question: What qualities make a devotee dear to the Lord according to the Gita?

hi.gold_answer: जो व्यक्ति किसी भी अस्तित्व से नफरत नहीं करता है, वह मित्रवत, दयालु, अहंकार से मुक्त, सुख और पीड़ा में संतुलित, क्षमाशील, हमेशा संतुष्ट, आत्म-नियंत्रित और मन और तर्क में समर्पित है, वह भगवान को प्रिय है (12.13-12.14)।
hi.question: गीता के अनुसार कौन से गुण एक भक्त को भगवान को प्रिय बनाते हैं?

sa.gold_answer: यः न अस्तित्वं द्वेष्यति, सः मैत्रीपूर्णः, करुणामयः, अहङ्कारमुक्तः, सुखे दुःखे च सन्तुलितः, क्षमाशीलः, सदा सन्तुष्टः, आत्मनियन्त्रितः, मनसि विवेके च समर्पितः, सः भगवन्तं प्रियः अस्ति (12.13-12.14)।
sa.question: गीतानुसारं किं लक्षणं भक्तं भगवन्तं प्रियं करोति?

_gold_src.text_en: He who beareth no ill-will to any being, friendly and compassionate, without attachment and egoism, balanced in pleasure and pain, and forgiving,
_gold_src.original: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
_gold_src.translit: adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamī

_gold_src.text_en: Ever content, harmonious, with the self-controlled, resolute, with mind and Reason dedicated to Me, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
_gold_src.original: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
_gold_src.translit: santuṣhṭaḥ satataṁ yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He from whom the world doth not shrink away, who doth not shrink away from the world, freed from the anxieties of joy, anger, and fear, he is dear to me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate cha yaḥ harṣhāmarṣha-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa cha me priyaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who wants nothing, is pure, expert, passionless, untroubled, renouncing every undertaking, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: anapekṣhaḥ śhuchir dakṣha udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ sarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

gold_answer: Freedom is not won by abstaining from work (3.4); none can remain actionless even a moment, driven by nature's qualities (3.5); so one should perform prescribed action, since action surpasses inaction (3.8).
question: Why does the Gita argue that no one can truly abstain from action, and that action is better than inaction?

hi.gold_answer: काम से दूर रहने से स्वतंत्रता नहीं मिलती है (3.4); कोई भी व्यक्ति प्रकृति के गुणों से प्रेरित होकर एक पल भी कर्महीन नहीं रह सकता है (3.5); इसलिए व्यक्ति को निर्धारित कार्य करना चाहिए, क्योंकि कार्य निष्क्रियता से आगे निकल जाता है (3.8)।
hi.question: गीता में यह तर्क क्यों दिया गया है कि कोई भी वास्तव में कर्म से दूर नहीं रह सकता है, और यह कि कर्म निष्क्रियता से बेहतर है?

sa.gold_answer: कर्मनिवृत्तेः कारणात् स्वातन्त्र्यं न प्राप्यते (3.4); प्रकृत्याः गुणैः प्रेरितः कश्चन क्षणः अपि कर्महीनः भवितुं न शक्नोति (3.5); अतः व्यक्तिः विहितं कार्यं कर्तुम् अर्हति, यतः कर्म निष्क्रियतां अतिक्रमयति (3.8)।
sa.question: गीता किमर्थं तर्कयति यत् कर्मात् यथार्थतया केनापि विमुखः भवितुं न शक्नोति, तथा च निष्क्रियतायाः अपेक्षया कर्म श्रेष्ठः इति?

_gold_src.text_en: Man winneth not freedom from action by abstaining from activity, nor by mere renunciation doth he rise to perfection.
_gold_src.original: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते। न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
_gold_src.translit: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣhkarmyaṁ puruṣho ’śhnute na cha sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigachchhati

_gold_src.text_en: Nor can anyone, even for an instant, remain really actionless; for helplessly is everyone driven to action by the qualities born of nature.
_gold_src.original: न हि कश्िचत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्। कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
_gold_src.translit: na hi kaśhchit kṣhaṇam api jātu tiṣhṭhatyakarma-kṛit kāryate hyavaśhaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛiti-jair guṇaiḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Perform thou right action, for, action is superior to inaction, and, inactive, even the maintenance of thy body would not be possible.
_gold_src.original: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः। शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
_gold_src.translit: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hyakarmaṇaḥ śharīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: In this world there is a twofold path, as I before said, O sinless one: that of yoga by knowledge, of the Sâñkhyas; and that of yoga by action, of the Yogis.
_nearmiss_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ। ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: śhrī bhagavān uvācha loke’smin dvi-vidhā niṣhṭhā purā proktā mayānagha jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But who, controlling the senses by the mind, O Arjuna, with the organs of action without attachment, performeth yoga by action, he is worthy.
_nearmiss_src.original: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन। कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yas tvindriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśhiṣhyate

gold_answer: Faith is threefold by nature — pure, passionate, and dark (17.2); each person's faith conforms to their nature, and a person is what their faith is (17.3).
question: How does the Gita relate a person's faith to their inherent nature?

hi.gold_answer: विश्वास स्वभाव से तीन गुना है-शुद्ध, भावुक और अंधेरा (17.2); प्रत्येक व्यक्ति का विश्वास उनके स्वभाव के अनुरूप है, और एक व्यक्ति वही है जो उनका विश्वास है (17.3)।
hi.question: गीता किसी व्यक्ति के विश्वास को उसकी अंतर्निहित प्रकृति से कैसे जोड़ती है?

sa.gold_answer: विश्वासः स्वभावतः त्रिगुणः अस्ति-शुद्धः, भावपूर्णः, कृष्णः च (17.2); प्रत्येकस्य व्यक्तेः विश्वासः स्वप्रकृत्या अनुरूपः भवति, तथा च व्यक्तिः एव तस्य विश्वासः भवति (17.3)।
sa.question: गीतायां व्यक्तेः विश्वासः तस्य अन्तर्निहितप्रकृत्या कथं सम्बद्धः अस्ति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Threefold is by nature the inborn faith of the embodied—pure, passionate, and dark. Hear thou of these.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा।सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha tri-vidhā bhavati śhraddhā dehināṁ sā svabhāva-jā sāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tāṁ śhṛiṇu

_gold_src.text_en: The faith of each is shaped to his own nature, O Bhârata. The man consists of his faith; that which his faith is, he is even that.
_gold_src.original: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत।श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārata śhraddhā-mayo ‘yaṁ puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Pure men worship the Gods; the passionate the gnomes and giants; the others, the dark folk, worship ghosts and troops of nature-spirits.
_nearmiss_src.original: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः।प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣha-rakṣhānsi rājasāḥ pretān bhūta-gaṇānśh chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Arjuna said: Those that sacrifice full of faith, but casting aside the ordinances of the Scriptures, what is verily their condition, O Krishna? Is it one of Purity, Passion, or Darkness?
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाचये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha ye śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ teṣhāṁ niṣhṭhā tu kā kṛiṣhṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ

gold_answer: Beings arise from food, food from rain, rain from sacrifice, and sacrifice from action (3.14); action springs from Brahma, so the all-pervading Eternal is ever present in sacrifice (3.15).
question: How does the Gita describe the cosmic cycle linking food, rain, sacrifice, and action?

hi.gold_answer: जीव भोजन से उत्पन्न होते हैं, वर्षा से भोजन, यज्ञ से वर्षा, और कर्म से बलिदान (3,14); क्रिया ब्रह्मा से उत्पन्न होती है, इसलिए सर्वव्यापी शाश्वत बलिदान में हमेशा मौजूद रहता है (3.15)।
hi.question: गीता भोजन, वर्षा, त्याग और कर्म को जोड़ने वाले ब्रह्मांडीय चक्र का वर्णन कैसे करती है?

sa.gold_answer: भोजनात् जीवः, वृष्ट्या अन्नं, यज्ञात् वृष्टिं, कर्म्मात् यज्ञं च उत्पद्यन्ते (3,14); कर्मः ब्रह्मात् उद्भवति, अतः सर्वव्यापीः सनातनः सर्वदा यज्ञे उपस्थितः भवति (3.15)।
sa.question: अन्नं, वृष्टिं, यज्ञं, क्रियां च सम्बद्धं विश्वचक्रं गीता कथं वर्णयति?

_gold_src.text_en: From food creatures become; from rain is the production of food; rain proceedeth from sacrifice; sacrifice ariseth out of action.
_gold_src.original: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः। यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
_gold_src.translit: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Know thou that from Brahma action groweth, and Brahma from the Imperishable cometh. Therefore the Eternal , the all-permeating, is ever present in sacrifice.
_gold_src.original: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्। तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣhara-samudbhavam tasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣhṭhitam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The righteous, who eat the remains of the sacrifice, are freed from all sins; but the impious, who dress food for their own sakes, they verily eat sin.
_nearmiss_src.original: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः। भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yajña-śhiṣhṭāśhinaḥ santo muchyante sarva-kilbiṣhaiḥ bhuñjate te tvaghaṁ pāpā ye pachantyātma-kāraṇāt

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who on earth doth not follow the wheel thus revolving, sinful of life and rejoicing in the senses, he, O son of Pritha, liveth in vain.
_nearmiss_src.original: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः। अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: evaṁ pravartitaṁ chakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati

gold_answer: The harmonized yogi sees the Self in all beings and all beings in the Self, seeing the same everywhere (6.29), and judges pleasure and pain everywhere by the likeness of his own self (6.32).
question: What does the Gita teach about the yogi who sees the same Self in all beings?

hi.gold_answer: सामंजस्यपूर्ण योगी आत्मा को सभी प्राणियों में और सभी प्राणियों को आत्मा में देखता है, हर जगह एक जैसा देखता है (6.29), और हर जगह सुख और दर्द का न्याय अपने स्वयं के स्वरूप से करता है (6.32)।
hi.question: गीता उस योगी के बारे में क्या सिखाती है जो सभी प्राणियों में एक ही आत्मा को देखता है?

sa.gold_answer: सामञ्जस्यपूर्णः योगी सर्वेषु प्राणीषु आत्मानं पश्यति तथा च सर्वेषु प्राणिषु आत्मानं पश्यति, सर्वत्र समानं पश्यति (6.29), सर्वत्र स्वस्वरूपेण सुखदुखस्य च न्यायनिर्णयः करोति (6.32)।
sa.question: सर्वप्राणेषु समानात्मं द्रष्टुं योगिनः विषये गीता किं शिक्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The self, harmonised by yoga, seeth the Self abiding in all beings, all beings in the Self ; everywhere he seeth the same.
_gold_src.original: सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि। ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni chātmani īkṣhate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśhanaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: He who, through the likeness of the Self , O Arjuna, seeth equality in everything, whether pleasant or painful, he is considered a perfect Yogî.
_gold_src.original: आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन। सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं सः योगी परमो मतः ॥
_gold_src.translit: ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśhyati yo ’rjuna sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who seeth Me everywhere, and seeth everything in Me, of him will I never lose hold, and he shall never lose hold of Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति। तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yo māṁ paśhyati sarvatra sarvaṁ cha mayi paśhyati tasyāhaṁ na praṇaśhyāmi sa cha me na praṇaśhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sages look equally on a Brâhmana adorned with learning and humility, a cow, an elephant, and even a dog and an outcaste.
_nearmiss_src.original: विद्याविनयसंपन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि। शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini śhuni chaiva śhva-pāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśhinaḥ

gold_answer: Whoever remembers the Lord while leaving the body attains him (8.5); whatever state one dwells on at the end, that one reaches (8.6); therefore one should remember the Lord at all times and fight (8.7).
question: What does the Gita teach about the state of mind at the moment of death and its effect on one's destination?

hi.gold_answer: जो कोई भी शरीर को छोड़ते समय भगवान को याद करता है, वह उसे प्राप्त करता है (8.5); अंत में कोई भी जिस स्थिति में रहता है, वह पहुँच जाता है (8.6); इसलिए व्यक्ति को हर समय भगवान को याद करना चाहिए और लड़ना चाहिए (8.7)।
hi.question: मृत्यु के समय मन की स्थिति और किसी के गंतव्य पर इसके प्रभाव के बारे में गीता क्या सिखाती है?

sa.gold_answer: यः कश्चित् शरीरात् निर्गत्य भगवन्तं स्मरति सः तं प्राप्नुयात् (8.8); अन्ते यः स्थितिः निवसति सा स्थितिः प्राप्यते (8.6); अतः सर्वदा भगवन्तं स्मृत्य युद्धं कर्तव्यम् (8.7)।
sa.question: मृत्योः क्षणे मनःस्थितिः, तस्य गन्तव्यं प्रति तस्य प्रभावः च विषये गीता किं शिक्षयति?

_gold_src.text_en: And he who, casting off the body, goeth forth thinking upon Me only at the time of the end, he entereth into My being: there is no doubt of that.
_gold_src.original: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्। यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
_gold_src.translit: anta-kāle cha mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Whosoever at the end abandoneth the body, thinking upon any being, to that being only he goeth, O Kaunteya, ever to that conformed in nature.
_gold_src.original: यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम्। तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥
_gold_src.translit: yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ tyajatyante kalevaram taṁ tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Therefore at all times think upon Me only and fight. With mind and Reason set on Me, without doubt thou shalt come to Me.
_gold_src.original: तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च। मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu mām anusmara yudhya cha mayyarpita-mano-buddhir mām evaiṣhyasyasanśhayam

_nearmiss_src.text_en: "Aum!" the one syllabled Eternal , reciting, thinking upon Me, he who goeth forth, abandoning the body, he goeth on the highest path.
_nearmiss_src.original: ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन्। यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: oṁ ityekākṣharaṁ brahma vyāharan mām anusmaran yaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṁ sa yāti paramāṁ gatim

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who constantly thinketh upon Me, not thinking ever of another, of him I am easily reached, O Pârtha, of this ever-harmonised Yogî.
_nearmiss_src.original: अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः। तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: ananya-chetāḥ satataṁ yo māṁ smarati nityaśhaḥ tasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ

gold_answer: Relinquishment is giving up the fruit of all actions (18.2); the pure relinquisher performs prescribed duty while abandoning attachment and fruit (18.9); since embodied beings cannot give up action entirely, the true relinquisher is one who renounces its fruit (18.11).
question: How does the Gita distinguish true relinquishment (tyaga) as renunciation of the fruits of action rather than of action itself?

hi.gold_answer: त्याग सभी कार्यों के फल को त्यागना है (18.2); शुद्ध त्याग करने वाला आसक्ति और फल को त्यागते हुए निर्धारित कर्तव्य का पालन करता है (18.9); क्योंकि मूर्त प्राणी पूरी तरह से कर्म नहीं छोड़ सकते हैं, सच्चा त्याग करने वाला वह है जो उसके फल को त्याग देता है (18.11)।
hi.question: गीता सच्चे त्याग (त्याग) को कर्म के बजाय कर्म के फल के त्याग के रूप में कैसे अलग करती है?

sa.gold_answer: त्यागः सर्वकार्याणां फलानां त्यागः अस्ति (18.2); शुद्ध-त्यागकः आसक्तिं फलं च परित्यज्य विहितं कर्तव्यं निर्वहति (18.9); यतः मूर्त-जीवः कर्मं पूर्णतया परित्यक्तुं न शक्नोति, अतः यथार्थ-त्यागकः एव तस्य फलस्य त्यागः भवति (18.11)।
sa.question: गीता सत्यत्यागं (त्यागः) कर्मस्य अपेक्षया कर्मफलस्य त्यागः इति कथं विभेदयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Sages have known as renunciation the renouncing of works with desire; the relinquishing of the fruit of all actions is called relinquishment by the wise.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः। सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha kāmyānāṁ karmaṇāṁ nyāsaṁ sannyāsaṁ kavayo viduḥ sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ prāhus tyāgaṁ vichakṣhaṇāḥ

_gold_src.text_en: He who performeth a prescribed action, saying, "it ought to be done," O Arjuna, relinquishing attachment and also fruit, that relinquishment is regarded as pure.
_gold_src.original: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन।सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
_gold_src.translit: kāryam ity eva yat karma niyataṁ kriyate ‘rjuna saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalaṁ chaiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Nor indeed can embodied beings completely relinquish action; verily he who relinquisheth the fruit of action, he is said to be a relinquisher.
_gold_src.original: न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः।यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥
_gold_src.translit: na hi deha-bhṛitā śhakyaṁ tyaktuṁ karmāṇy aśheṣhataḥ yas tu karma-phala-tyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Verily renunciation of actions that are prescribed is not proper; the relinquishment thereof from delusion is said to be of darkness.
_nearmiss_src.original: नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते।मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: niyatasya tu sannyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But even these actions should be done leaving aside attachment and fruit, O Pârtha; that is my certain and best belief.
_nearmiss_src.original: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च।कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalāni cha kartavyānīti me pārtha niśhchitaṁ matam uttamam

gold_answer: Matter and spirit are named the Field and its Knower (13.1); the body is the Field and its knower is the Knower of the Field (13.2); the Lord is the Knower in all Fields, and knowing both is true wisdom (13.3).
question: What distinction does the Gita draw between the Field and the Knower of the Field?

hi.gold_answer: द्रव्य और आत्मा को क्षेत्र और उसका ज्ञाता कहा जाता है (13.1); शरीर क्षेत्र है और उसका ज्ञाता क्षेत्र का ज्ञाता है (13.2); भगवान सभी क्षेत्रों में ज्ञाता है, और दोनों को जानना ही सच्चा ज्ञान है (13.3)।
hi.question: गीता में क्षेत्र और क्षेत्र जानने वाले के बीच क्या अंतर है?

sa.gold_answer: द्रव्यस्य आत्मस्य च नाम क्षेत्रं तथा तस्य ज्ञाता (13.1); शरीरं क्षेत्रं तथा तस्य ज्ञाता क्षेत्रं ज्ञाता (13.2); भगवतः सर्वेषु क्षेत्रेषु ज्ञाता, उभयोः ज्ञानं च सत्यज्ञानम् (13.3)।
sa.question: क्षेत्रज्ञस्य क्षेत्रज्ञस्य च मध्ये गीता किं भेदं कल्पयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Arjuna said: Matter and Spirit, even the Field and the Knower of the Field, wisdom and that which ought to be known, these I fain would learn, O Keshava.
_gold_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च। एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
_gold_src.translit: arjuna uvācha prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva kṣhetraṁ kṣhetra-jñam eva cha etad veditum ichchhāmi jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ cha keśhava

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: This body, son of Kuntî, is called the Field; that which knoweth it is called the Knower of the Field by the Sages.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha idaṁ śharīraṁ kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyate etad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Understand Me as the Knower of the Field in all Fields, O Bhârata. Wisdom as to the Field and the Knower of the Field, that in My opinion is the wisdom.
_gold_src.original: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत। क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
_gold_src.translit: kṣhetra-jñaṁ chāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhārata kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama

_nearmiss_src.text_en: What that Field is and of what nature, how modified, and whence it is, and what He is and what His powers, hear that now briefly from Me.
_nearmiss_src.original: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: tat kṣhetraṁ yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yat sa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Thus the Field, Wisdom and the Object of Wisdom, have been briefly told. My devotee, thus knowing, enters into My Being.
_nearmiss_src.original: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: iti kṣhetraṁ tathā jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ choktaṁ samāsataḥ mad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate

gold_answer: Sensations of heat and cold, pleasure and pain come and go and must be borne patiently (2.14); the one unshaken by them, balanced in pain and pleasure, is fit for immortality (2.15).
question: How does the Gita connect the endurance of pleasure and pain to the attainment of immortality?

hi.gold_answer: गर्मी और ठंड, सुख और दर्द की संवेदनाएँ आती हैं और जाती हैं और उन्हें धैर्य से सहन करना चाहिए (2.14); जो उनके द्वारा विचलित नहीं होता, दर्द और सुख में संतुलित होता है, वह अमरता के योग्य होता है (2.15)।
hi.question: गीता सुख और पीड़ा की सहनशीलता को अमरता की प्राप्ति से कैसे जोड़ती है?

sa.gold_answer: उष्णस्य शैत्यस्य, सुखस्य, दुःखस्य च संवेदनाः आगच्छन्ति, गच्छन्ति, सहनशीलाः भवितव्याः च (2.14); यस्मात् सः अचञ्चलः, दुःखेन सुखेन सन्तुलितः, सः अमरत्वाय योग्यः भवति (2.15)।
sa.question: सुख-दुःखयोः सहिष्णुतां अमरत्वस्य प्राप्त्या सह गीता कथं संयोजयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The contacts of matter, O son of Kuntî, giving cold and heat, pleasure and pain, they come and go, impermanent; endure them bravely, O Bhârata.
_gold_src.original: मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः। आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥
_gold_src.translit: mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ āgamāpāyino ’nityās tans-titikṣhasva bhārata

_gold_src.text_en: The man whom these torment not, O chief of men, balanced in pain and pleasure, steadfast, he is fitted for immortality.
_gold_src.original: यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ। समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
_gold_src.translit: yaṁ hi na vyathayantyete puruṣhaṁ puruṣharṣhabha sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛitatvāya kalpate

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As the dweller in the body experienceth in the body childhood, youth, old age, so passeth he on to another body; the steadfast one grieveth not thereat.
_nearmiss_src.original: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा। तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Taking as equal pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat, gird thee for the battle; thus thou shalt not incur sin.
_nearmiss_src.original: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ। ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛitvā lābhālābhau jayājayau tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi

gold_answer: Defensible answers differ: the call to act without attachment to fruits (2.47), the final surrender of all duties to the Lord (18.66), or fixing the mind on God in loving devotion (9.34).
question: Which single verse best captures the central teaching of the Bhagavad Gita?

hi.gold_answer: रक्षात्मक उत्तर अलग-अलग हैंः फलों के प्रति लगाव के बिना कार्य करने का आह्वान (2.47), भगवान को सभी कर्तव्यों का अंतिम समर्पण (18.66), या प्रेमपूर्ण भक्ति में भगवान पर मन को स्थिर करना (9.34)।
hi.question: कौन सा एकल श्लोक भगवद गीता की केंद्रीय शिक्षा को सबसे अच्छी तरह से दर्शाता है?

sa.gold_answer: प्रतिरक्षणीयानि उत्तरानि भिन्नाः सन्ति-फलानां प्रति आसक्तिं विना कार्यं कर्तुं आह्वानम् (2.47), सर्वकार्याणां अन्तिमं समर्पणम् (18.66), अथवा प्रेमपूर्णभक्त्या ईश्वरं प्रति मनस्य स्थिरीकरणं (9.34)।
sa.question: भगवद्गीतायाः केन्द्रीयशिक्षां कः एकवाक्यं सर्वोत्तमरूपेण ग्रहीयते?

_gold_src.text_en: Thy business is with the action only, never with its fruits; so let not the fruit of action be thy motive, nor be thou to inaction attached.
_gold_src.original: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
_gold_src.translit: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi

_gold_src.text_en: Abandoning all duties come unto Me alone for shelter; sorrow not, I will liberate thee from all sins.
_gold_src.original: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: On Me fix thy mind, be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; prostrate thyself before Me; harmonised thus in the Self , thou shalt come unto Me, having Me as thy supreme goal.
_gold_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु। मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
_gold_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Perform action, O Dhananjaya, dwelling in union with the divine, renouncing attachments and balanced evenly in success and failure: equilibrium is called yoga.
_nearmiss_src.original: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय। सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate

gold_answer: It is arguable both ways: the Gita says action-yoga is better than renunciation (5.2) and urges performing action (3.8), yet also teaches that the goals of Sankhya and Yoga are one (5.5).
question: Does the Gita ultimately favor the path of action or the path of renunciation?

hi.gold_answer: यह दोनों तरीकों से तर्क योग्य हैः गीता कहती है कि कर्म-योग त्याग से बेहतर है (5.2) और कर्म करने का आग्रह करती है (3.8), फिर भी यह भी सिखाती है कि सांख्य और योग के लक्ष्य एक हैं (5.5)।
hi.question: क्या गीता अंततः कर्म के मार्ग या त्याग के मार्ग का समर्थन करती है?

sa.gold_answer: इदं उभयतः वादयोग्यम् अस्ति-गीता वदति यत् त्यागस्य अपेक्षया कर्मयोगः उत्तमः (5.2) तथा कर्मं कर्तुं प्रेरयति (3.8), तथापि सांख्यस्य योगस्य च लक्ष्यानि एकानि (5.5) इति अपि शिक्षयति।
sa.question: किं गीता अन्ततः कर्ममार्गं वा त्यागमार्गं वा समर्थयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Renunciation and yoga by action both lead to the highest bliss; of the two, yoga by action is verily better than renunciation of action.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाच संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ। तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī bhagavān uvācha sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaśh cha niḥśhreyasa-karāvubhau tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśhiṣhyate

_gold_src.text_en: Perform thou right action, for, action is superior to inaction, and, inactive, even the maintenance of thy body would not be possible.
_gold_src.original: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः। शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
_gold_src.translit: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hyakarmaṇaḥ śharīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: That place which is gained by the Sânkhyas is reached by the Yogîs also. He seeth, who seeth that the Sânkhya and the Yoga are one.
_gold_src.original: यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते। एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
_gold_src.translit: yat sānkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṁ tad yogair api gamyate ekaṁ sānkhyaṁ cha yogaṁ cha yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Arjuna said: Renunciation of actions Thou praisest, O Krishna, and then also yoga. Of the two which one is the better? That tell me conclusively.
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाच संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि। यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्िचतम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha sannyāsaṁ karmaṇāṁ kṛiṣhṇa punar yogaṁ cha śhansasi yach chhreya etayor ekaṁ tan me brūhi su-niśhchitam

gold_answer: Defensible readings differ: 18.66 seems to set duty aside for surrender, yet 18.65 and 3.30 frame surrender as the proper spirit in which duty is still performed.
question: When Krishna tells Arjuna to 'abandon all duties' and take refuge in him, does this negate the Gita's earlier emphasis on doing one's duty?

hi.gold_answer: रक्षात्मक रीडिंग अलग-अलग होती हैः ऐसा लगता है कि 18.66 समर्पण के लिए कर्तव्य को अलग रखता है, फिर भी 18.65 और 3 फ्रेम समर्पण उचित भावना के रूप में जिसमें कर्तव्य अभी भी किया जाता है।
hi.question: जब कृष्ण अर्जुन को'सभी कर्तव्यों को छोड़ने'और उनकी शरण लेने के लिए कहते हैं, तो क्या यह गीता के पहले अपने कर्तव्य को करने पर जोर देने को नकारता है?

sa.gold_answer: रक्षणीयाः पठनानि भिन्नानि भवन्तिः 18.66 समर्पणार्थं कर्तव्यं पृथक् स्थापयति इति प्रतीयते, तथापि 18.65 तथा च 3:30 फ्रेम्-समर्पणम् इति उचितात्मारूपेण यत्र कर्तव्यं अद्यापि क्रियते।
sa.question: यदा कृष्णः अर्जुनं "सर्वकार्याणि परित्यज्य" तस्य आश्रयं ग्रहीतुं वदति, तदा एतत् गीतायाः कर्तव्यपालनस्य पूर्वप्राधान्यं निराकरोति वा?

_gold_src.text_en: Abandoning all duties come unto Me alone for shelter; sorrow not, I will liberate thee from all sins.
_gold_src.original: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Merge thy mind in Me, be my devotee, sacrifice to Me, prostrate thyself before Me, thou shalt come even to Me. I pledge thee My troth; thou art dear to Me.
_gold_src.original: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
_gold_src.translit: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo ‘si me

_gold_src.text_en: Surrendering all actions to Me, with thy thoughts resting on the supreme Self , from hope and egoism freed, and of mental fever cured, engage in battle.
_gold_src.original: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा। निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
_gold_src.translit: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ

_nearmiss_src.original: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन।न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: idaṁ te nātapaskyāya nābhaktāya kadāchana na chāśhuśhruṣhave vāchyaṁ na cha māṁ yo ‘bhyasūtayi

gold_answer: Defensible answers include 9.30-9.31, where even a great sinner who turns to the Lord is accounted righteous and swiftly attains peace, and 18.66, where the Lord promises to free the surrendered one from all sins.
question: Which verse most strongly expresses the idea of divine grace overriding a person's past sins?

hi.gold_answer: रक्षात्मक उत्तरों में शामिल हैं 9.30-9.31, जहाँ एक महान पापी जो भगवान की ओर मुड़ता है उसे भी धर्मी माना जाता है और जल्दी से शांति प्राप्त होती है, और 18.66, जहाँ भगवान समर्पण करने वाले को सभी पापों से मुक्त करने का वादा करते हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक किसी व्यक्ति के पिछले पापों पर हावी होने वाली दिव्य कृपा के विचार को सबसे अधिक दृढ़ता से व्यक्त करता है?

sa.gold_answer: रक्षणीयेषु उत्तरेषु 9.30-9.31 इत्येतानि अन्तर्भवन्ति, यत्र भगवन्तं प्रति गच्छतः महान् पापी अपि धार्म्मिकः इति गण्यते तथा च शीघ्रं शान्तिं प्राप्नुयात्, तथा च 18.66, यत्र भगवतः शरणागतं सर्वं पापात् मुक्तुं प्रतिज्ञां करोति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः व्यक्तेः अतीतस्य पापानाम् उपरि दैविककृपेः विचारं सर्वाधिकं प्रबलतया प्रकटयति?

_gold_src.text_en: Even if the most sinful worship Me, with un divided heart, he too must be accounted righteous, for he hath rightly resolved;
_gold_src.original: अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक्। साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥
_gold_src.translit: api chet su-durāchāro bhajate mām ananya-bhāk sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Speedily he becometh dutiful and goeth to eternal peace; O Kaunteya, know thou for certain that My devotee perisheth never.
_gold_src.original: क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति। कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
_gold_src.translit: kṣhipraṁ bhavati dharmātmā śhaśhvach-chhāntiṁ nigachchhati kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśhyati

_gold_src.text_en: Abandoning all duties come unto Me alone for shelter; sorrow not, I will liberate thee from all sins.
_gold_src.original: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
_gold_src.translit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The same am I to all beings; there is none hateful to Me nor dear. They verily who worship Me with devotion, they are in Me, and I also in them.
_nearmiss_src.original: समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः। ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: samo ’haṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo ’sti na priyaḥ ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpyaham

gold_answer: Both are defensible: a literal reading rests on the battlefield context and 11.33's command to fight, while a metaphorical reading draws on 2.18 (the Self is never slain) and 3.30 (surrender all acts to the Lord and fight).
question: Should Krishna's command to 'fight' be read as a literal call to war or as a metaphor for inner spiritual struggle?

hi.gold_answer: दोनों रक्षात्मक हैंः एक शाब्दिक पठन युद्ध के मैदान के संदर्भ और लड़ने के लिए 11.33 के आदेश पर निर्भर करता है, जबकि एक रूपक पठन 2,18 (आत्मा कभी नहीं मारा जाता है) और 3:30 (सभी कार्यों को भगवान को सौंप दें और लड़ें) पर आधारित है।
hi.question: क्या कृष्ण के'लड़ने'के आदेश को युद्ध के शाब्दिक आह्वान के रूप में या आंतरिक आध्यात्मिक संघर्ष के रूपक के रूप में पढ़ा जाना चाहिए?

sa.gold_answer: उभौ रक्षणीयाः सन्ति-शाब्दिकपाठः युद्धक्षेत्रस्य सन्दर्भे तथा युद्धस्य 11.33 इत्यस्य आदेशे आश्रितः अस्ति, यदा तु रूपकपाठः 2.18 (आत्मः कदापि न म्रियते) तथा च 3:30 (सर्वं कृत्यं भगवन्तं समर्प्य युद्धं कुर्वन्तु) इत्येतयोः आश्रितः अस्ति।
sa.question: कृष्णस्य'युद्धस्य'आदेशः युद्धस्य शाब्दिक-आह्वानरूपेण वा आन्तरिक-आध्यात्मिक-संघर्षस्य रूपकरूपेण वा पठनीयः भवेत् वा?

_gold_src.translit: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śharīriṇaḥ anāśhino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata

_gold_src.text_en: Surrendering all actions to Me, with thy thoughts resting on the supreme Self , from hope and egoism freed, and of mental fever cured, engage in battle.
_gold_src.original: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा। निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
_gold_src.translit: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Therefore stand up! win for thyself renown, Conquer thy foes, enjoy the wealth filled realm, By Me they are already overcome, Be thou the outward cause, left-handed one.
_gold_src.original: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्। मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥
_gold_src.translit: tasmāt tvam uttiṣhṭha yaśho labhasva jitvā śhatrūn bhuṅkṣhva rājyaṁ samṛiddham mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva nimitta-mātraṁ bhava savya-sāchin

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Slain, thou wilt obtain heaven; victorious, thou wilt enjoy the earth; therefore stand up, O son of Kuntî, resolute to fight.
_nearmiss_src.original: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम्। तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: hato vā prāpsyasi swargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣhyase mahīm tasmād uttiṣhṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛita-niśhchayaḥ

gold_answer: Defensible answers differ: the yogi is greater than ascetics and the wise (6.46), but the most united is the devotee absorbed in the Lord (6.47, 12.2).
question: What is the highest form of yoga according to the Gita?

hi.gold_answer: रक्षात्मक उत्तर अलग-अलग हैंः योगी तपस्वियों और बुद्धिमानों से बड़ा है (6,46), लेकिन सबसे अधिक एकजुट भगवान में लीन भक्त है (6,47,12.2)।
hi.question: गीता के अनुसार योग का सर्वोच्च रूप कौन सा है?

sa.gold_answer: प्रतिरक्षणीयानि उत्तरानि भिन्नाः सन्ति-योगी तपस्विभ्यः ज्ञानिभ्यः च श्रेष्ठः अस्ति (6.46), परन्तु भगवाने निमग्नः भक्तः एव सर्वाधिकः संयुक्तः अस्ति (6.47,12.2)।
sa.question: गीतानुसारं योगस्य सर्वोच्चं रूपं किम्?

_gold_src.text_en: The Yogî is greater than the ascetics; he is thought to be greater than even the wise; the Yogî is greater than the men of action; therefore become thou a Yogî, O Arjuna!
_gold_src.original: तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः। कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥
_gold_src.translit: tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogī jñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥ karmibhyaśh chādhiko yogī tasmād yogī bhavārjuna

_gold_src.text_en: And among all Yogîs, he who, full of faith, with the inner Self abiding in Me, adoreth Me, he is considered by Me to be the most completely harmonised.
_gold_src.original: योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना। श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥
_gold_src.translit: yoginām api sarveṣhāṁ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā śhraddhāvān bhajate yo māṁ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ

_gold_src.text_en: The Blessed Lord said: They who with mind fixed on Me ever harmonised worship Me, with faith supreme endowed, these, in My opinion, are best in yoga.
_gold_src.original: श्री भगवानुवाचमय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha mayy āveśhya mano ye māṁ nitya-yuktā upāsate śhraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Arjuna said: Those devotees who ever harmonised worship Thee, and those also who worship the Indestructible, the Unmanifested, whether of these is the more learned in yoga?
_nearmiss_src.original: अर्जुन उवाचएवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते।येचाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: arjuna uvācha evaṁ satata-yuktā ye bhaktās tvāṁ paryupāsate ye chāpy akṣharam avyaktaṁ teṣhāṁ ke yoga-vittamāḥ

gold_answer: It can be read either way: 4.13 says the four classes were created by a distribution of qualities and actions, 18.41 ties duties to one's inborn qualities, and 18.47 elevates one's own duty regardless.
question: Does the Gita present social class (varna) as a fixed birth-order or as grounded in qualities and actions?

hi.gold_answer: इसे किसी भी तरह से पढ़ा जा सकता हैः 4.13 कहता है कि चार वर्ग गुणों और कार्यों के वितरण द्वारा बनाए गए थे, 18.41 कर्तव्यों को किसी के जन्मजात गुणों से जोड़ता है, और 18.47 किसी भी तरह से अपने कर्तव्य को बढ़ाता है।
hi.question: क्या गीता सामाजिक वर्ग (वर्ण) को एक निश्चित जन्म-क्रम के रूप में प्रस्तुत करती है या गुणों और कार्यों में आधारित है?

sa.gold_answer: एतत् उभयतः अपि पठितुं शक्यतेः 4.13 वदति यत् चत्वारः वर्गाः गुणानाम् क्रियायाः च वितरणेन निर्मिताः इति, 18.41 कर्तव्यानि स्वजन्मगुणैः सह सम्बद्धाः इति, 18.47 तु स्वकर्तव्यं उत्तोलयति इति।
sa.question: किं गीता सामाजिकवर्गं (वर्णं) निश्चित-जन्म-क्रमरूपेण वा गुणेषु क्रियासु च आधाररूपेण उपस्थापयति?

_gold_src.text_en: The four castes were emanated by Me, by the different distribution of qualities and actions; know Me to be the author of them, though the actionless and inexhaustible.
_gold_src.original: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः। तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: chātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛiṣhṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśhaḥ tasya kartāram api māṁ viddhyakartāram avyayam

_gold_src.text_en: Of Brâhmanas, Kshattriyas, Vaishyas and Shûdras, O Parantapa, the duties have been distributed, according to the qualities born of their own natures.
_gold_src.original: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप।कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
_gold_src.translit: brāhmaṇa-kṣhatriya-viśhāṁ śhūdrāṇāṁ cha parantapa karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāva-prabhavair guṇaiḥ

_gold_src.text_en: Better is one's own duty though destitute of merits than the well-executed duty of another. He who doeth the duty laid down by his own nature incurreth not sin.
_gold_src.original: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
_gold_src.translit: śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt svabhāva-niyataṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣham

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Ploughing, protection of kine, and trade are the Vaishya duty, born of his own nature. Action of the nature of service is the Shûdra duty, born of his own nature.
_nearmiss_src.original: कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम्।परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥
_nearmiss_src.translit: kṛiṣhi-gau-rakṣhya-vāṇijyaṁ vaiśhya-karma svabhāva-jam paricharyātmakaṁ karma śhūdrasyāpi svabhāva-jam

gold_answer: Defensible choices include the garment simile of rebirth (2.22), the inevitability that makes grief pointless (2.27), and the promise that remembering the Lord at death leads to him (8.5).
question: Which verse offers the most consoling view of death?

hi.gold_answer: रक्षात्मक विकल्पों में पुनर्जन्म का परिधान उपमा (2.22), अनिवार्यता जो दुःख को व्यर्थ बनाती है (2.27), और यह वादा कि मृत्यु के समय भगवान को याद करना उनकी ओर ले जाता है (8.5) शामिल हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक मृत्यु के बारे में सबसे सांत्वना देने वाला दृष्टिकोण प्रस्तुत करता है?

sa.gold_answer: रक्षणीयेषु विकल्पेषु पुनर्जन्मस्य वस्त्रस्य उपाख्यानम् (2.22), दुःखम् निष्प्रयोजनं कर्तुम् अनिवार्यं (2.27), मृत्योः समये भगवन्तं स्मरणं तं प्रति प्रेरयति (8.5) इति प्रतिज्ञां च अन्तर्भवन्ति।
sa.question: कः श्लोकः मृत्योः विषये सर्वाधिकं आश्वासकदृश्यं प्रददाति?

_gold_src.text_en: As a man, casting off worn-out garments, taketh new ones, so the dweller in the body, casting off worn-out bodies, entereth into others that are new.
_gold_src.original: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि। तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
_gold_src.translit: vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇi tathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānya nyāni sanyāti navāni dehī

_gold_src.text_en: For certain is death for the born, and certain is birth for the dead; therefore over the inevitable thou shouldst not grieve.
_gold_src.original: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च। तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
_gold_src.translit: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛityur dhruvaṁ janma mṛitasya cha tasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi

_gold_src.text_en: And he who, casting off the body, goeth forth thinking upon Me only at the time of the end, he entereth into My being: there is no doubt of that.
_gold_src.original: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्। यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
